programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan investigates the case of his old friend Kent Morrissey, who mysteriously dropped dead while carrying out an operation. Guest starring Jack Klugman

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The co-pilot of a plane carrying Dr Sloan to a conference is murdered by persons unknown mid-flight, and the remainder of the crew members find themselves struck down by a mystery illness. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: A startled Kimble discovers that the man giving him a ride is a wanted criminal when a police roadblock looms up in front of them

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Two brothers running a bowling alley are up against a woman determined to shut them down, but things get far worse when one of them is accused of murder.

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part one of two. A legendary Texas Ranger is back to seek justice for his murdered son, and to help track down an assassin. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man's mistress is found murdered, adding a further unpleasant dimension to his already acrimonious divorce - and before the CSIs can begin their investigation, another body turns up. A suspect is eventually identified, and the case goes to court, but a wily defence attorney manages to have Delko, whose evidence is vital, ruled unfit to testify

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A bomb detonates at a military charity concert, killing two men. Called to investigate, Tony and McGee try to determine whether the intended target was the 1980s rocker headlining the act or if the attack was part of a larger scheme against the military. Meanwhile, Jimmy prepares for parenthood. Keith Carradine guest stars, with Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble finds himself becoming emotionally involved with a co-worker who bears a strong resemblance to his late wife

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: Perry stops by to visit a friend's son stationed at an army base in Switzerland, and must defend the young man when he is accused of killing a fellow soldier

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team inadvertently crashes a bank robbery in progress and soon find themselves taken hostage. Meanwhile, Sylvester's feelings for Florence grow

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Chuck Norris fights crime and helps the community as the eponymous Walker. Trivette is ridiculed as he turns to a psychic for help in a kidnapping case

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs is taken aback to discover Tony's father is linked to the team's latest investigation, a politically sensitive case involving an assassination attempt on Prince Sayif, a visiting Saudi dignitary with a taste for the high life. Guest starring Robert Wagner, with Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: A naval officer involved in an illegal underground street race is killed when his vehicle explodes. The team's investigations reveal he was carrying classified equipment in relation to his work on a new form of battery that the military had hoped to adopt, and that the explosion - captured on CCTV - is not in accordance with that expected of normal fuel. Meanwhile, Callen latest driving misdemeanour means he must attend traffic school. Chris O'Donnell stars

programme.desc: The abrasive, unpopular host of a money-raising telethon is murdered live on air, and it is not long before suspicion falls on his former writers. Medical detective drama, guest starring Dom DeLuise, with Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Dr Sloan's elderly cousin Jonathan Nash decides to bequeath his million-dollar fortune to Community General Hospital - but later dies in mysterious circumstances. Drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The agents are asked to help Nasa with a top-secret rocket launch, but they must sabotage their own mission when Walter's date finds a bomb strapped to her chest

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine's teeth and fingertips are found in a pickpocket's stolen purse, and Ducky discovers he had been tortured on a rack before dying. The agents realise he may have been killed in error, and that the real target was his ex-girlfriend, a skilled player of computer games who unwittingly uncovered a mysterious code. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents learn one of their former investigative assistants has been murdered, and had been selling information about how to rob a warehouse full of valuable possessions belonging to a woman convicted of fraud. As the case proceeds, Tony becomes infatuated with the new leader of another team, who apparently returns his feelings. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homicide police hand the case of a retired marine colonel found murdered with an axe over to NCIS when they are unable to solve the case, despite all the circumstantial evidence pointing to the deceased's drug-addicted son. They discover the suspect's mother went missing two years previously and Ziva suggests he may have killed more than one parent, while the family's neighbour is revealed to also have had a motive for wanting the man dead. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly and Cote de Pablo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a marine working for the Defence Intelligence Agency who was blamed for the disappearance of 500 specialised rifles in Afghanistan. A cryptic message found at the scene, written in the victim's blood, leads the agents on the trail of a controversial manuscript that claims to expose the truth about the missing weapons, and an FBI agent with a secret. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents examine the death of a seaman and find themselves working alongside Barrett. Gibbs makes his displeasure about the circumstances of the case well known, prompting the rest of the investigators to worry that he might be about to leave the NCIS again. Meanwhile, Ziva introduces her new boyfriend to her team-mates. Crime drama, guest starring Enrique Murciano

programme.title: NCIS

Drama, starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate a government organisation dedicated to child prodigies while researching the death of a marine whose body appeared in the middle of a field disguised as a scarecrow, and realise a 12-year-old could be the killer's next target. Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Cote de Pablo, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette star

Crime drama, guest starring Ralph Waite (The Waltons), with Mark Harmon

programme.desc: After a mid-series break, the drama following the exploits of the Los Angeles-based Office of Special Projects returns. Sam and Callen investigate when five men wearing US Marine uniforms are found dead in a warehouse and discover they were killed by a lethal toxin that could be used as a bioweapon. Initial inquiries lead them to a former military chemist who now has Alzheimer's, while Callen recognises one of the victims as someone who used to work as hired muscle for the well-connected owner of a strip joint. Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J star

programme.title: CSI: Miami

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man on death row for murder is given a stay of execution amid concerns over the DNA evidence used at his trial - but Horatio remains convinced he is guilty and sets out to find fresh proof. As the case is re-examined, the CSIs receive a call about a new double killing that looks suspiciously like the investigation they are working on

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A bomb goes off in a nightclub, killing the perpetrator in the process. The team examines the body, and discovers traces of radioactive material, sparking suspicion that another device has been planted somewhere in the city. It transpires the bomber was stabbed before the explosion - and Horatio's supposedly dead half-brother could have been involved

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Gang members discover Horatio is planning a crackdown on their smuggling operation and decide the simplest and most direct way to ensure he fails is by ordering his murder. Meanwhile, new lab technician Natalia Boa Vista proves to have a markedly different agenda from the rest of the team

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a girl killed in a blaze on board her family's yacht. Meanwhile, Calleigh clashes with her replacement in the ballistics lab

To Gene's relief, the pathologist's report appears to put everyone in the clear, but when the truth about the criminal's demise emerges, it is up to the 21st-century cop to decide whether to reveal it. John Simm and Philip Glenister star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Sandra reopens an investigation into the suspected suicide of a Foreign Office diplomat, whose body was found in a frozen London lake after she had suffered the personal tragedy of a miscarriage and the professional embarrassment of having a government laptop stolen from her home. The woman's fiance and twin sister both believe her death is connected to sensitive information held on the missing computer and blame her boss for her downfall - and when Sandra clashes with the man himself, it only makes her more determined to pursue the conspiracy theory, despite Strickland's advice to tread carefully. Simon Day and Sharon Small guest star

Guest starring Gina McKee, Ruth Gemmell and Alexander Siddiq

Medieval mystery, starring Derek Jacobi

programme.desc: The duo are invited to help create an Arabian-style garden in Malaga. Before long, a local resident is found dead, and a mysterious landowner is the chief suspect. When it transpires that someone has blocked the mountainside channels on which the new garden will rely for its supply of water, it appears the precious commodity may provide a motive for the killing. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Rosemary & Thyme
programme.desc: Laura and Rosemary are hired to re-create a Victorian garden in Regent's Park, where they meet all manner of intriguing characters - but the oasis of calm in central London is shattered by the discovery of a body. The victim is a respected Harley Street psychiatrist - whose three-legged dog appears to hold the key to solving the case. Mystery drama, starring Felicity Kendal and Pam Ferris

programme.title: Father Brown

Fifties-set crime drama based on the stories by GK Chesterton, starring Mark Williams as a Roman Catholic priest who attempts to solve various misdemeanours

programme.desc: WPC Gina Dawson is beginning to enjoy being under cover as Ruby, but struggles with her growing feelings for Chris. Police drama set in the 1950s, starring Jennie Jacques and Charlie De'Ath

Starring Honeysuckle Weeks, Perdita Weeks, Val McLane, Alec Newman, Crispin Bonham-Carter and Patrick Ryecart

programme.desc: A Christian Scientist couple refuse medicine for their newborn baby, leaving Sister Julienne questioning the nature of absolute faith. programme.desc: A diabetic teenager faces a tough time after falling pregnant, a situation complicated by her boyfriend's criminal past. programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste pick up the case of a man who lost thousands of pounds and his home after falling in love with a woman claiming to be a gold trader. The story takes a bizarre twist as the mysterious woman known as Maryanne starts making financial demands of them too

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Kent Police officers risk their lives to rescue an individual trapped on a cliff face and West Midlands Police track down a man in possession of a gun

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams find potentially profitable items at the East of England showground in Peterborough, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp attempts to get to the bottom of an ancient mystery

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: A simple missing person's case takes a dramatic turn as Frank and Lu find themselves in the middle of a dispute over the opening of a new Shakespeare Centre where it's clear that no one is what they seem. Simon Williams and Josie Lawrence guest star

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King head off into the heart of traditional Japan to find out about the historic roots of the nation's cuisine. Dave puts his Strictly fancy footwork into action by kneading noodle dough the artisan way, before the duo then take an awe-inspiring journey to see Mount Fuji, spend a night at a Buddhist temple and are entertained by one of Kyoto's geisha complete with traditional drinking games

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin embarks on a property hunting mission in Oxfordshire with a young couple who are both medical professionals and have a sizable £1.5million budget to find a country home for their growing family. Away from potential properties, Jonnie braves the Oxfordshire waters during an exhilarating attempt at punt racing

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades makes a useful magnetic tool organiser, while Dom Chinea creates a planter complete with decorative house numbers. For the big build the pair vie to make the most coveted of household features - the kitchen island. From the archive, Rachel de Thame digs up an unwanted garden tree and Gay Search reveals a clever way to turn an unappealing side return into an attractive outdoor space

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Carl from Stoke brings in a key that turns out to be not quite what it appears. Alice, from Wales, tries to sell a moulded model advertisement for men's underwear from the early 20th century. Naz from Halifax presents a harmonica that she wants to sell so she and her young son can have a holiday, and Sharon from Hellsby tries to interest the dealers in a Victorian silver belt. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a co-host round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former world champion boxer Anthony Crolla, Commonwealth silver medallist high jumper Morgan Lake, former British tennis number one Greg Rusedski and Olympic medal-winning swimmer Steve Parry. Sue Barker hosts

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: After a day spent house-hunting, the newlyweds take up an invitation from Nessa and Smithy to relax with a trip to the bowling alley. However, when tensions between Gavin and Stacey reach a head at the end of the night, their two friends begin to regret making the offer

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey is fuming that Lily has ordered glam holiday stuff on her credit card and vows to make Ruby pay for it. Dotty pitches her idea to a distracted Ruby, who eventually agrees to allow Dotty and Tiff to host tables. Lola is fed up about Isaac, Kim offers to match Mila next, and Tiff and Keegan celebrate their new room at Sonia's

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Contestants Kate and Jonathan and brothers Kurt and Eren answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Kate Winslet, who talks about her role in romantic Victorian drama Ammonite. Accomplished actor Stanley Tucci discusses his role as a writer suffering dementia in Supernova, Pirates of the Caribbean's Orlando Bloom chats about powerful revenge drama Retaliation, while rising British star Kingsley Ben-Adir talks about his part in sci-fi anthology Soulmates. Plus, Silk City featuring Ellie Goulding perform New Love

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Cameras eavesdrop on more tense, confrontational, flirty and often emotional reunions. Geordie Shore star Nathan Henry is desperate to know if ex Tommy was only with him for the fame. Tallia Storm finally reveals the real reason she dumped racing driver Seb Morris six years ago. Will they be able to clear the air and try again, or will the truth leave their exes in exile?

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: The series rounds off with former Apprentice candidate Ryan-Mark Parsons finding out why he was fired from his relationship by Oskars, while actor and broadcaster Mim Shaikh is served up some shocking home truths by his ex, Destiny

programme.title: Tropic Thunder (2008)
programme.desc: A director's epic Vietnam movie is behind schedule and over budget. Taking the advice of the grizzled war veteran on whose life the film is based, he drops the actors into a jungle with the intention of filming them fighting guerrilla-style - but the cast is unaware the surrounding warfare is genuine. Comedy, directed by and starring Ben Stiller, with Jack Black, Steve Coogan and, in an Oscar-nominated role, Robert Downey Jr

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty presents a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: This week Saturday Kitchen is hosted by Matt Tebbutt and he's joined by chefs Dr Rupy Aujla and Georgina Hayden, plus special guest Emily Atack. Drinks expert Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: As the chef's Cornish odyssey nears its end, he takes a break in the city of Truro, where he discovers an unusual ghost story and tours the magnificent cathedral. He also meets a family passionate about offal, who prepare Rick a meal of pickled ox tongue and beef heart tacos, and in the county's most northerly parish he tells the story of an eccentric vicar who would retrieve the bodies from nearby shipwrecks and bury them in his church graveyard

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United, Leicester City v Arsenal and Chelsea v Manchester United

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: Ben and Marina Fogle present this month's Lifeline appeal on behalf of the charity Tommy's, who fund pioneering research into miscarriage, stillbirth and premature birth, as well as provide pregnancy health information to parents.

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the latest meeting of the campaign in Madrid. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades is at Altrincham Recycling Centre in Greater Manchester, where expert Duncan McKean and furniture Chloe Kempster transform an old radiogram and a chest of drawers

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Northampton that will allow two children with limited mobility access to the outdoor space

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v England (Kick-off 4.45pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the match in the third round of fixtures, held at Principality Stadium. Wales won just one match in last year's championship, but have already bettered that total this time around following narrow victories over Ireland and Scotland, while England bounced back from a shock opening day defeat at home to the Scots with a comfortable triumph over Italy. With analysis from Jeremy Guscott, Martin Johnson and Sam Warburton, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Brian Moore

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In this episode, Rhys and Stephanie, a couple from Tamworth in Staffordshire, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Connie takes it upon herself to get involved in Grace's love life and confronts Leon. Meanwhile, Faith and Lev do everything in their power to keep their marriage together, and Jan is horrified to find her son is in danger, but struggles to accept what she can do to save him. Starring Amanda Mealing and Emily Carey

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton implies that Dryden was embroiled in a wider conspiracy, and the discovery of video recordings made by the witness while in protective custody appears to confirm this. Arnott uncovers evidence that a tracker had been planted on one of the convoy vehicles, and when Denton is given special dispensation to visit her dying mother, the AC-12 officers realise too late how much danger she may be in. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Leeds United v Aston Villa at Elland Road. The Citizens were looking to reinforce their status as title-favourites against a West Ham side that have performed well this term, while the Villans were hoping to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of Leeds at Villa Park in October. Plus, West Bromwich Albion v Brighton & Hove Albion and Newcastle United v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: Invictus (2009)
programme.desc: Following his election as president of South Africa, Nelson Mandela finds himself the leader of a racially and economically divided nation, and seeks a means to unite the people. The country's entry in the 1995 Rugby World Cup provides a perfect opportunity, and he enlists the aid of captain Francois Pienaar in making his team an inspiration to all South Africans. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Morgan Freeman and Matt Damon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Leeds United v Aston Villa at Elland Road. The Citizens were looking to reinforce their status as title-favourites against a West Ham side that have performed well this term, while the Villans were hoping to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of Leeds at Villa Park in October. Plus, West Bromwich Albion v Brighton & Hove Albion and Newcastle United v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: A service for the second Sunday of Lent

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Fulham (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the Premier League clash at Selhurst Park. Palace have won the last three meetings between the sides, including a 2-1 win at Craven Cottage in October, but Fulham will be determined to end that run of defeats as they are battling to avoid relegation despite some encouraging recent results on their travels

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from Witley Recycling Centre, including a cooker hood and a collection of old jars

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets a couple who have already sold up and are desperate to invest £425,000 in their perfect home in Somerset

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani is taken on a tour of one of London's cemeteries, as she discovers a trove of treasures right on her doorstep and sets out to crack the mystery of a non-native bird. Adam Henson shows how a super-meal is specially concocted for his animals during the winter months, and Tom Heap investigates the controversial decision that allows UK farmers to use a banned pesticide on their fields

programme.title: Attenborough's Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the many ways animals use colour, with cameras built specifically for this series revealing a world normally invisible to human eyes. The peacock's tail has evolved entirely to impress females and is one of the most extreme colour ornaments in the natural world, while mandrill baboons signal their strength and status with striking red and blue face masks, the brighter the colours, the higher ranking the male

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where items assessed include a rare artefact that survived the attack on Pearl Harbour and an LS Lowry painting. There are also ancient carvings from the Indian subcontinent and an intriguing wooden carving with a mysterious past

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: Protocol will not let Tom Brannick near the island investigation, but driven by his hunger to settle a score from his past, the detective attempts to solve the kidnapping case. With the help of Birdy he is soon in perilous pursuit of a possible connection to the crime, but the question soon arises whether it is Goliath he is hunting, or someone making a false trail. Crime thriller, starring James Nesbitt

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's five Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Manchester United at Stamford Bridge and Sheffield United v Liverpool at Bramall Lane. The Blues and United drew 0-0 when the sides met at Old Trafford in October, but both clubs will have been hoping to claim all three points on this occasion as they looked to boost their hopes of at least finishing in the top four. The Blades have struggled so far this season, but will have drawn hope from Liverpool suffering a number of poor results against bottom-six teams this term. Plus, Crystal Palace v Fulham, Leicester City v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Burnley

programme.title: The Rugby Codebreakers
programme.desc: Carolyn Hitt presents a look at the Welsh rugby players during the 100-year amateur era who broke what was considered a taboo by many to switch codes from union to league. Carolyn travels to Huddersfield where in 1895, members of 22 teams decided to resign from the amateur English Rugby Union, with the professional rugby league being born as a result. She also provides an insight into those from South Wales who found great success after crossing codes in the 1920s and the black players who flourished in the 1970s, including Roy Francis and Clive Sullivan

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John Baptiste hear from a man who started an online relationship with a woman in France, whose incessant pleas for money turned to horrifying threats

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Nick Bailey optimises a narrow garden space, the Flog It! team shares secrets on how to nab a bargain at a car boot sale, and a dowdy kitchen gets a new lease of life

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four treasured family heirlooms back to life. First up, Peter Mason from Herefordshire arrives with an old-fashioned ice-cream maker that belonged to his beloved Granny Ethel, and holds memories of happy times at her house churning vanilla ice cream together. Meanwhile, organ restorer David Burville takes on a Spanish barrel organ, known as an organillo. Later, the experts restore a portable 1950s record player and a leather document case

programme.title: Workout the Wright Way
programme.desc: On Feel Good Friday, Mark Wright revisits his favourite exercises from the week

programme.title: Curiosity
programme.desc: Paul Martin hosts as James and Simon take on Kim and Scott, going head to head to determine who will meet today's curious collector Tanya for a chance to win £1,000

programme.title: The Truth About Lions
programme.desc: Big Cat Diary presenter Jonathan Scott and Professor Craig Packer conclude their investigation into the creatures' social habits. They examine the use of the mane, the role of murderous stepfathers in the lion's family life, and the challenges the animals face for survival at a time when their presence has become restricted to small parts of Africa

programme.title: Put Your Money Where Your Mouth Is
programme.desc: With £1,000 to spend and half their items going to auction, it remains to be seen whether Kate Bliss and Caroline Hawlsy will be able to turn a profit at all. Kate banks on a pair of designer shoes and Caroline puts her faith in a 17th-century chest. It's a tense race to the finish as they discover the week's overall winner

programme.title: Life
programme.desc: The focus turns to apes and monkeys, revealing their similarities to humans. Large armies of hamadryas baboons battle on the plains of Ethiopia to steal females and settle old scores, while Japanese macaques beat the cold by lounging in thermal springs. An orang-utan baby fails in its struggle to make an umbrella out of leaves, and capuchins try to smash nuts with a large rock. Narrated by David Attenborough

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk is joined by chef Anna Haugh in the historic kitchens of one of the UK's finest stately homes to cook food enjoyed at some of the royal family's favourite haunts - dishes they have enjoyed with family and friends, at picnics and in restaurants. Anna creates a meat pudding that would have found favour with Bertie, Prince of Wales, and his mistress Lillie Langtry and cooks up a mille feuilles dessert from the recipe book of a Buckingham palace kitchen maid, hidden in the Royal Archive for over 100 years. Plus, historian Dr Polly Russell visits the restaurants given the royal seal of approval in the 1950s and '60s

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: A collection of interesting and previously unseen finds from the show's travels around the country, with visits to Margam Country Park in south Wales, Highcliffe Castle in Dorset, Sandon Hall in Staffordshire and Powderham Castle in Devon. The antiques experts include Catherine Southon, Adam Partridge, Philip Serrell, Mark Stacey, Claire Rawle and David Fletcher, with items ranging from some folk art Scandinavian nutcrackers to a Napoleonic prisoner of war handcrafted box. Presenter Paul Martin also takes a look around the stately Firle Place in East Sussex

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Ronni Ancona, Nathan Caton, Colin Murray and Sally Phillips go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games for the final time, prior to the week's overall winner being declared

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons hosts the quick-fire quiz in which six contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions

programme.title: Inside the Zoo
programme.desc: It is training time for barn owls Rowan and Cedar, recently arrived from Colchester Zoo, while senior keeper Erika patiently attempts to win the confidence of Sumatran tigress Dharma. At Highland Wildlife Park, a member of the public enjoys the company of Arktos and Walker the male polar bears when she takes part in a Keeper for the Day experience

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team look back at some of the floral highlights of 2020. It is a celebration of some of the star flowers of gardens, from tulips and roses to salvias and dahlias. There is also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their own gardens throughout the year

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: The presenter treks through the southern fjords of Norway to find the stunning Oseberg ship. This lost 1,000-year-old vessel transformed the fierce reputation of the Vikings and became a symbol of a nation's fight for freedom

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: Pioneer of the ever-growing contemporary London jazz scene, Moses Boyd started playing drums aged 13. His affinity for a wide range of music, from grime and electronica, to the gospel, soul and funk he heard growing up in south London have heavily influenced his unique sound. He joins the host to discusses his musical journey, loves and influences, illustrated by classic clips from the show's archives. Jools also catches up with legendary American folk musician Peggy Seeger, who performs All in the Mind, and Rag'n'Bone Man, who gives a first performance of his new single All You Ever Wanted

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Faisal Islam, the BBC's Economics Editor, reviews the day's important national and international news stories

programme.title: Empire of the Sun (1987)
programme.desc: An English boy living in Shanghai is separated from his parents during the Japanese invasion and forced to live on the streets. The youngster is befriended by an American sailor, who helps the boy adapt to life without his family, before the pair are eventually captured and subjected to the terrors of internment. Steven Spielberg's Second World War drama based on JG Ballard's semi-autobiographical novel, starring Christian Bale, John Malkovich and Nigel Havers

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: While on the algae planet Tyrol discovers the Temple of Five, and the Cylons take an interest in the find as it may lead to a return to Earth. Athena learns her baby Hera may still be alive, and Lee requires assistance from Anders. Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber star

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: Admiral Adama faces a life-changing decision when he has to decide whether to keep the Eye of Jupiter, which could ultimately lead mankind to Earth, or destroy it to prevent the Cylons from finding it. Edward James Olmos, Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber star

programme.title: Trump Takes On the World
programme.desc: After one of the most shocking presidencies in history, Donald Trump's top advisers and the leaders who clashed with him lift the lid on the critical moments of his foreign policy. The first edition looks back over Trump's first year and a half in office, when he fell out spectacularly with some of America's oldest allies, and offers an inside look at crucial meetings and summits where the US President shocked his counterparts

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: In south Wales, sisters Chloe and Kelsey plan the muddiest wedding in the show's history. There's a zorb rugby match in a 20,000-seat stadium, surprise rugby superstars and Mum getting launched into the air to catch the ball

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The den-builders come to the aid of 12-year-old Will, who had to give up playing rugby after developing Langerhans cell hystiocytosis, and is seeking a way to stay in the game

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: We celebrate some of the Blue Peter viewers' best and bravest achievements by awarding the highest accolade that Blue Peter can give - the Gold Blue Peter badge. And we find out which celebrity is awarded this coveted award.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads to Gabon in search of crocodiles, chimpanzees and feisty elephants

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Rocket Dog's pups receive their first lesson in desert survival, the Whiskers family moves burrows and a power struggle breaks out among the Commandos. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: Males Wilson and Phillippe try to find themselves mates and burrows they can call home. Narrated by Bill Nighy

programme.title: Pooch Perfect
programme.desc: In the semi-final, the last four competitors face a breed that judge Colin Taylor is world-famous for grooming - the lamb-like Bedlington terrier. In Dogology, crossbreed puppies enter the salon and the groomers must identify the breeds of their two parents. One of the most dramatic transformations takes place on the Dogwalk when the dogs take the shape of completely different animals, to the delight of their owners. Presented by Sheridan Smith

programme.title: Around the World in 80 Gardens
programme.desc: The horticultural tour continues with a trip to South Africa, one of the greenest zones in the world. In Cape Town, Monty Don visits the renowned Kirstenbosch Botanic Gardens, where he encounters the country's national plant, the king protea, and discovers an abundance of plant life in the Drakensberg mountains

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice (1940)
programme.desc: A couple are keen to find suitable husbands for their five daughters. When a wealthy gentleman and his friend arrive in the area, it leads to an encounter between headstrong daughter Elizabeth and aloof gentleman Mr Darcy, and their instant dislike gradually turns into an unexpected romance. Period drama based on Jane Austen's novel, starring Laurence Olivier and Greer Garson

programme.title: Jane Austen: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Historian Lucy Worsley embarks on a road trip across England to take in some of the places Jane Austen lived in and visited during her lifetime. She aims to demonstrate how Jane's personal experiences influenced the central themes of home and property in her novels, and makes use of detective-work to recreate some of the most important locations that no longer stand. She begins in Stoneleigh Abbey, Warwickshire, retracing a journey that marked one of the many times the Austens veered wildly between wealth and genteel poverty - mirroring the Dashwoods of Sense and Sensibility. Lucy also travels from Jane's birthplace in Steventon, Hampshire, to her brother's grand estate at Godmersham Park - the inspiration for Darcy's Pemberley in Pride and Prejudice, along with Lyme Regis, Bath and Southampton

programme.title: Brideshead Revisited (2008)
programme.desc: A student at Oxford in the 1920s becomes fascinated with the glamorous lives of an aristocratic friend and his sister, but a complex love triangle develops between the three, while their idyllic relationship is threatened by the arrival of the siblings' embittered mother. Drama based on Evelyn Waugh's novel, starring Matthew Goode, Ben Whishaw, Hayley Atwell and Emma Thompson

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Highcliffe Castle in Devon, where Philip Serrell and Adam Partridge uncover a piece of modern design by Edward Paolozzi and a Second World War ration pack

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The six remaining designers are put into teams of two and challenged to transform three shops - a pet accessories shop, a camera store, and a kitchen equipment retailer. Each designer must make a pitch to the business owner they have been assigned to, meaning that three contenders will get their idea approved, while the others act as their assistants. Helping Michelle Ogundehin decide who should stay or go this week is guest judge and pop-up retail guru Ross Bailey

programme.title: Perry and Croft: Made in Britain
programme.desc: The concluding episode of the documentary celebrating the work of writing duo David Croft and Jimmy Perry examines how Dad's Army, It Ain't Half Hot Mum, Hi-de-Hi! and You Rang, M'Lord? all encapsulated the end of an era, reflecting old ideas as they faded while a new British identity was being forged

programme.title: My Hero
programme.desc: In the second of an occasional series in which comedians reminisce about those who have inspired them, Outnumbered star Hugh Dennis pays an affectionate tribute to Ronnie Barker. He follows the comedy actor's career path from the London stage to TV, where he met sometime partner Ronnie Corbett, with whom he made some of the most fondly remembered sketches of all time. But he was also a solo star in such shows as Porridge and Open All Hours, as well as the writer of many of his own scripts. Hugh unearths some of these original documents and meets people who worked with him to deliver a fresh appreciation of one of Britain's most unassuming comedy greats

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Prime Minister Winston Churchill is to visit Walmington-on-Sea, and there is a shooting contest between Captain Mainwaring's unit and a rival platoon from nearby Eastgate to see who will have the honour of guarding him. Classic comedy first aired in 1968, guest starring Barbara Windsor, with Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Made in Scotland
programme.desc: First in a two-part documentary following the acclaimed comedian, actor and musician, as he returns to the country of his birth and reveals the influences and motivations that helped him move on from being a welder in Glasgow to become 'The Big Yin'. The programme features deeply personal interviews with Billy himself, alongside contributions from some of the greatest names in comedy and popular culture, who talk about Connolly's lasting influence

programme.title: Billy Connolly: Made in Scotland
programme.desc: Second of a two-part documentary following the comedian, as he returns to the country of his birth and reveals the influences and motivations that helped him become 'The Big Yin'. The programme finds Billy back in the Scotland of his childhood, where he reveals that knitted woolly swimming trunks were not a figment of his imagination. Featuring contributions by friends, fans and colleagues including Eddie Izzard, Ross Noble, Micky Flanagan, AL Kennedy, Tracey Ullman, Val McDermid, Sharleen Spiteri and Eddi Reader

programme.title: Apocalypse Now: The Final Cut (1979)
programme.desc: An army captain is sent on a top-secret mission into Cambodia, where his goal is to find and assassinate a renegade colonel who has gone insane and is worshipped by his followers as a god. However, as his patrol boat journeys further upriver, the conflict and disarray surrounding him becomes increasingly disturbing and nightmarish. Extended version of Francis Ford Coppola's acclaimed Vietnam War drama - re-edited in 2019 by the director himself to mark the film's 40th anniversary. Starring Martin Sheen, Marlon Brando and Robert Duvall

programme.title: Frantz (2016)
programme.desc: A German woman mourning her fiance's death in the First World War is slowly drawn to the French soldier who also visits his grave. Romantic drama, starring Pierre Niney, Paula Beer and Ernst Stotzner. In French and German

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the Bloomers from Mablethorpe in Lincolnshire, who have been competing in Bloom for 10 years but have never won the top prize. With three ambitious new projects planned this year, they hope to not only win their first ever gold award, but also to put their forgotten seaside town back on the map and encourage tourists to visit

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Horticulturist Christine Walkden takes to the skies above Cumbria, visiting Levens Hall where she lends a hand planting spring bulbs, and hears some personal memories of a Lakeland lad. She then travels to Holehird to meet the volunteers who have restored a 19th-century garden to its former glory, before discovering the spur of Beatrix Potter's writing career

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don and the team look back at some of the floral highlights of 2020. It's a celebration of some of the star flowers of gardens, from tulips and roses to salvias and dahlias. There's also another chance to see what viewers were getting up to in their own gardens last year

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Tom Heap explores Northamptonshire's Nene Valley, finding out how a farmer who was fighting flooding is now embracing wetland wildlife to make his land pay. Adam Henson hopes for good news from his ewes as he discovers how many are in lamb, while Charlotte Smith investigates whether Brexit is delivering all it promised for the UK's fishing industry and ecologist Josh Styles reveals the secret world inside a prehistoric plant

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway visits north Wales, where his walk takes him from the village of Abergwyngregyn to the quay at Conwy. This walk also has him heading to the hills, trying his hand at golf and learning how a top secret operation played a major part in winning the Second World War

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Your Garden Made Perfect
programme.desc: Suzanne and Andrew have a huge problem in their lives - a giant derelict swimming pool in their back garden in Plymouth. A succession of landscaping companies have run a mile the moment they gaze into the abyss, and their budget does not even stretch to simply filling it in - so how will designers Tom Massey and Pip Probert transform this unique outdoor space? Meanwhile, Joel Bird comes to the help of an Essex couple with a nightmare patio. Presented by Angela Scanlon

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and John Cameron help two teams of couples find bargains at an antiques fair in the Royal Highland Centre in Edinburgh, with Anita Manning overseeing proceedings

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Ahead of St David's Day, Sean Fletcher explores beautiful Tintern Abbey in Monmouthshire, and Welsh Rugby legends share their stories of finding faith after hanging up their boots

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: In Ushaw, County Durham, Catherine Southon examines a toy speedboat and James Lewis discovers a rare poster that came from a morgue. Paul Martin looks at rare Catholic artefacts

programme.title: This Beautiful Fantastic (2016)
programme.desc: A young woman who dreams of being a children's author forges an unlikely friendship with a cantankerous, rich old widower, as well as his housekeeper and an inventor who frequents the library where she works. Drama, starring Jessica Brown Findlay, Tom Wilkinson, Andrew Scott and Jeremy Irvine

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Half of the group head off to the hill stations of the Nilgiri mountains, but the drive involves a treacherous climb that has them on the edge of their seats. Dennis and Paul complete the journey by steam train, passing a station full of monkeys before arriving in Ooty, a town that allows Dennis to fulfil a lifelong dream, as he gets to play a game on the very first snooker table. Sheila, Amanda and Lionel attend a Hindu ancestor ceremony held to remember departed loved ones and reflect on death itself and the fact that they have lost many famous friends over the last year

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: With more than 100 boats in the harbour, Newlyn is Cornwall's biggest fishing port and the outcome of the Brexit trade negotiations could transform the future for its fleet on inshore fishing boats. In the face of an uncertain future, the fleet must prepare for modernisation and whatever challenges or opportunities Brexit may bring

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: The explorer travels the length of North and South America. The expedition begins at the northernmost point in Alaska, journeying south through Canada to the border with the US. In Denali National Park, he witnesses first hand the impact of climate change on a glacier. He also hunts caribou with native Americans, hears from both sides of the conflict over a controversial oil drilling plan, and discovers Vancouver's opioid addiction crisis

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As the series draws to a close, the farmers begin their preparations for the colder months ahead. Father and Daughter team, Frank and Georgia Hunter continue to find ways to adapt and survive on their 600-acre mixed livestock farm. Meanwhile in Northumberland, Ewan and Emma decide to find a new, bigger farm, and in the Lake District, Matt's vintage temperamental bailer keeps breaking down as he attempts to bring in the hay

programme.title: Chris Packham's Animal Einsteins
programme.desc: The presenter introduces a look at animals with impressive communication skills, discovering how white-throated sparrows use song to attract mates and the complex vocabulary of prairie dogs. The programme reveals how dolphins are able to address each other by name and chimpanzees use sign language, and also reveals species that communicate using ultrasound, vibrations and parts of the light spectrum beyond the ability of human sense to process

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, joined virtually by a diverse group of 20 members of the public drawn from all walks of life, who are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined by special guests Joe Lycett and Judi Love, who will be beamed into the studio to chew over the lighter end of the news spectrum

programme.title: Dylan Moran: Off the Hook
programme.desc: The broadcast premiere of a stand-up performance from the comedian's 2015 UK tour. Dylan Moran playfully scrambles through the struggles of life. He journeys through politics, loneliness and middle age, offering his uniquely kaleidoscopic lens on familiar subjects and distilling pearls of wisdom all along the way

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Scottish creativity on a journey from Oban to Glasgow. Travelling through the Highlands, Frankie visits the home of Grant Morrison, who has written stories for some of the comic world's most iconic characters. Resuming his trek, he travels from Dunoon to Bannockburn, the site of Robert the Bruce's most famous battle, where he learns how to fight with a sword and shield just like the Scots Knights of the 1300s. Frankie also witnesses young artists brightening up the darker corners of Glasgow and reflects on the city's shameful past, profiting off slavery in the British colonies

programme.title: The Company You Keep (2012)
programme.desc: A respected lawyer is forced to go on the run when a reporter exposes the truth about him - he is really a member of a radical militant group wanted for questioning in a murder case, who has been living under a fake identity for 30 years. As the fugitive goes in search of his old allies for sanctuary, the reporter delves into his past and uncovers disturbing secrets. Thriller based on the novel by Neil Gordon, directed by and starring Robert Redford, with Shia LaBeouf, Julie Christie and Susan Sarandon

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: The fight to stop Sylar has consequences that will change everything as Nathan tries to keep him from meeting the US president. Meanwhile, Hiro learns there are repercussions for regaining his ability, and Matt is forced to go to extraordinary lengths to protect his future with his family. Michael Dorn guest stars, with Zachary Quinto, Adrian Pasdar, Masi Oka and Greg Grunberg

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Dom's plans are frustrated when Ange blocks his discharge, and he may suffer extreme physical side effects when he takes matters into his own hands. Alex's blissful romance with Donna is rocked when a patient seems to know all about him. Nicky is forced to turn to Louis and Kian when her health issues return, but Cameron couldn't be less interested

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Katherine Jenkins: From Wales to the World
programme.desc: Since Neath girl Katherine Jenkins burst onto the music in 2003 she has had 13 number one albums, making her one of the world's best-selling classical singers. In a documentary originally shown to celebrate her 40th birthday in June 2020, the mezzo-soprano looks back over her career highlights, talks about hitting the big screen in her first feature film and reveals how she has kept busy during the lockdown, with weekly online performances for her fans and a special VE Day concert in an empty Royal Albert Hall. The programme also includes a tribute from Katherine to the NHS and key workers

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on September 6, with performances by artists including Adamski, Mariah Carey, Talk Talk, the Farm, Deee-Lite, Bombalurina, Caron Wheeler, Janet Jackson and Loose Ends

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on September 13, with performances by artists including Londonbeat, Sonia, Janet Jackson, INXS, Bass-O-Matic, Maria McKee, the KLF, Steve Miller Band and Faith No More

programme.title: St David's Day at the BBC
programme.desc: A rummage through the BBC's music archives, featuring a selection of tracks from some of the most important and innovative Welsh artists of the past few decades. The programme includes performances by Manic Street Preachers, Catatonia, Super Furry Animals, Marina and the Diamonds, Stereophonics, Feeder, Shakin' Stevens, Bonnie Tyler, Shirley Bassey and Tom Jones

programme.title: Tom Jones' 1950s: The Decade That Made Me
programme.desc: Tom Jones fronts the first of four retrospective documentaries in which celebrated musicians look back at the decades that defined them. For Tom, that decade is the 1950s, the era following the austerity of the Second World War that saw a boom in popular culture, which swept aside the old order and ushered in a new era of entertainment. The veteran singer provides a first-hand guide to his formative years in a small mining community in south Wales, revealing how he - much like many people of his generation - turned to TV, movies, radio and music to find a voice for himself. Revisiting Treforest and Pontypridd, where he spent his childhood and teenage years, Tom recalls his joy when rationing finally ended, his encounter with American GIs stationed near his home, and the arrival of rock 'n' roll and the 'Teddy Boys'. Includes contributions by writers Joan Bakewell, Katherine Whitehorn and Michele Hanson, and historians Alwyn Turner, Dr Martin Johnes, Tony Russell and Francis Beckett

programme.title: Dame Shirley Bassey: Electric Proms 2009
programme.desc: Trevor Nelson and Edith Bowman present highlights of the singer's performance to celebrate her 50 years in showbusiness. Classic tracks such as Big Spender and Goldfinger appear alongside songs from her album The Performance, with accompaniment by the BBC Concert Orchestra and guest collaborators David Arnold, James Dean Bradfield and Richard Hawley

programme.title: Radio 2 in Concert
programme.desc: Introduced by Fearne Cotton, the popular and successful Welsh rockers perform an intimate concert of tracks from their 2019 UK number one album Kind, alongside songs from their much-loved back catalogue including Dakota, Have a Nice Day and Mr Writer

programme.title: Our Musical History
programme.desc: DJ Trevor Nelson and singer-songwriter Corinne Bailey Rae present their ultimate soundtrack in an hour of classic and contemporary soul and R'n'B gems. Given an airing are childhood favourites such as the Jackson 5 and Gladys Knight. as well as inspirational tracks from Prince, Mary J Blige, Stevie Wonder and Marvin Gaye. As they watch their selection, the duo reveal the reasons behind their choices

programme.title: Tom Jones' 1950s: The Decade That Made Me
programme.desc: Tom Jones fronts the first of four retrospective documentaries in which celebrated musicians look back at the decades that defined them. For Tom, that decade is the 1950s, the era following the austerity of the Second World War that saw a boom in popular culture, which swept aside the old order and ushered in a new era of entertainment. The veteran singer provides a first-hand guide to his formative years in a small mining community in south Wales, revealing how he - much like many people of his generation - turned to TV, movies, radio and music to find a voice for himself. Revisiting Treforest and Pontypridd, where he spent his childhood and teenage years, Tom recalls his joy when rationing finally ended, his encounter with American GIs stationed near his home, and the arrival of rock 'n' roll and the 'Teddy Boys'. Includes contributions by writers Joan Bakewell, Katherine Whitehorn and Michele Hanson, and historians Alwyn Turner, Dr Martin Johnes, Tony Russell and Francis Beckett

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The team discover how the combination of increased leisure time and affordable rail transport brought a new kind of freedom for working-class Victorians. Ruth Goodman travels along the south Devon coast from Paignton to Kingswear, where she helps prepare a paddle steamer for a journey up the River Dart, while at Swanage, Peter Ginn finds out what it was like to work on the excursion trains and the impact mass tourism had on the area

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The adventurer travels along the Yangtze river into China, where he meets Mosuo singer Namu, who explains the region's traditional walking marriage ceremonies. He continues into India to marvel at one of Assam's great tea estates, makes the acquaintance of the local elephants and stays at a monastery where the monks introduce him to their unique dancing style

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Twelve years after the death of his grandson, Risto Kurtti receives a mysterious letter at his home in Helsinki. Finnish psychological thriller, starring Antti Virmavirta

programme.title: Man in Room 301
programme.desc: Convinced that Leo is Elias Leppo, Risto panics when he learns that his grandson Kalle has gone climbing with him. Unsure about Risto's theory, Eeva calls Seppo to try to find out if Elias is still in Finland. In Finnish

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the history of the Norse warriors, delving into their customs and searching for the truth behind their fearsome reputation. He begins by telling the story of their origins, and investigates the world of their ancient ancestors. The programme charts Scandinavia's prehistoric landscape, from the Baltic Sea to Norway's Atlantic coast, and Neil examines archaeological evidence to create a portrait of the society from which the Vikings emerged

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Jackie Brambles hosts the edition first aired on September 6, with performances by artists including Adamski, Mariah Carey, Talk Talk, the Farm, Deee-Lite, Bombalurina, Caron Wheeler, Janet Jackson and Loose Ends

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1990
programme.desc: Gary Davies hosts the edition first aired on September 13, with performances by artists including Londonbeat, Sonia, Janet Jackson, INXS, Bass-O-Matic, Maria McKee, the KLF, Steve Miller Band and Faith No More

programme.title: Britpop at the BBC
programme.desc: A look at the vibrant form of indie music known as Britpop, which in the 1990s stamped its presence onto the UK cultural scene and led to the notion of 'Cool Britannia', as a new wave of bands re-evaluated what it meant to be British. The movement was dominated by the battle between its biggest-selling exponents, Blur and Oasis, who in August 1995 did battle in a memorable bid for the number one spot. The programme also features the bands Elastica, Sleeper, Suede and Menswear

programme.title: Himalaya with Michael Palin
programme.desc: The adventurer travels along the Yangtze river into China, where he meets Mosuo singer Namu, who explains the region's traditional walking marriage ceremonies. He continues into India to marvel at one of Assam's great tea estates, makes the acquaintance of the local elephants and stays at a monastery where the monks introduce him to their unique dancing style

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead

programme.title: The Many Faces of
programme.desc: The story of a quiet, dedicated man who might have been a bank manager, but went on to become one of the nation's most popular comedy stars. Ben Elton, Michael Grade, David Renwick and Josephine Tewson are among friends and colleagues who remember his partnership with Ronnie Corbett in sketch show The Two Ronnies, as skinflint grocer Arkwright in Open All Hours and crafty prisoner Fletch in Porridge. There is also a look at his prolific writing career and his work as an actor in serious roles

programme.title: Britain's Ancient Capital: Secrets of Orkney
programme.desc: Andy Torbet dives below the waves in search of the inspiration for the first stone circle, while Chris Packham and Neil Oliver spend the night on an abandoned island as they hunt for clues as to why cultures change. Meanwhile, Shini Somara tests the technology behind a bronze age sauna and the archaeologists uncover a remarkable find

programme.title: Ireland to Sydney by Any Means
programme.desc: Adventurer Charley Boorman and his team look for alternative transport after finding themselves on a sinking cargo boat while en route to Borneo to help with a Unicef vaccination project. After flying to Bali, they embark on a series of journeys involving speedboats, overcrowded ferries and a hand-made boat to reach the shores of Australia. Once there, bad weather thwarts their attempt to take the most direct path to their final destination - Sydney

programme.title: Sorry Angel (2018)
programme.desc: Jacques, a successful writer and single father from Paris, begins an intense romance with Arthur, a young aspiring filmmaker. However, they soon face rejection and sickness. Drama, starring Vincent Lacoste, Pierre Deladonchamps and Denis Podalydès. In French with English subtitles

programme.title: Bute: The Scot Who Spent a Welsh Fortune
programme.desc: Suzanne Packer narrates the story of Victorian aristocrat John Patrick Crichton-Stuart, the third Marquess of Bute, who financed the construction of distinctive Welsh landmarks including the gothic Cardiff Castle, and Castell Coch. One of the richest men in his era, Lord Bute had a passion for architecture, but is also reported to have been a forward-thinking intellectual who supported women's right and striking miners, and Suzanne explores how Wales became the beneficiary of this visionary Scottish lord's extravagant tastes

programme.title: Mimi & Co
programme.desc: It’s Halloween and Mimi is on a mission to prank her best friend, Ashleigh, who claims she is not afraid of anything. She has found a spooky cave rumoured to be haunted by a ghost searching for his own heart, which was stolen several hundred years ago. Mimi also introduces sisters Angel, Melody and Nevaeh, who are super-proud of their traveller heritage and go trick or treating on their plot of land. Another Halloween fan is TikTok phenomenon Fats Timbo, who is Mimi’s Q&A guest and tells us how she has managed to use her platform to empower herself and others. Ashleigh is unimpressed when she finally arrives at the cave - until a surprise visitor gives her the fright of her life.

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Lauren Layfield hosts as two teams fight it out to dodge detention and put the cool back into school. In this episode, Mr Learner is training to be a PE teacher and, as usual, he has come over-prepared! Trying to win Lauren’s shiny gold stars are team captains Myles and Kathryn, They are joined by Blue Peter’s Lyndsey Russell and comedians Vikki Stone, Ria Lina and Nathan Caton as they all battle it out in a series of hilarious rounds guaranteed to make you laugh. Rounds include Kids Have Arted, Mimecraft, and everyone’s favourite, Pie the Supply! This week they are all pretending to be art teachers, but only one of them is telling the truth. So watch someone get pied in the face before the final round, High School Dropout, where someone from the teams gets binned from a great height!

We join Etti, who is having a fun day out with her family at some local gardens. They see lots of different flowers and animals including a big rabbit statue! But will these make Etti and her sister, Grace, smile? Meanwhile, Charlie and his family are out visiting a zoo where they see lots of different birds. Mum does an impression that makes Charlie smile, so his family use an instant camera to take some lovely family photos - but what will they do with them? After a quick stop at the local park, Etti and Grace decide to stop at a friend’s house to play peek-a-boo together. Grace creates her own happy faces with cut-out features. Finally we see what Charlie and his family were taking photos for - a photo album! What a fun and special way to look back on their family day out.

programme.title: My First
programme.desc: We follow Sophia and her family as they celebrate the Jewish holiday of Purim. After travelling across to the local kosher bakery, Sophia and her family are shown the traditional food Hamantaschen, before having a go at trying to make their own! Delicious! Once they finish up, it's time for her and her family to put together their own costumes ready to attend the synagogue. When they arrive, the local Jewish community are in full swing getting ready to celebrate the holiday festival of Purim. After decorating the traditional noise-makers, Sophia is ready to use it during the reading of the Megillah. This is where we finally get to learn the full story behind Purim and why it's so important to the Jewish community. Then comes Mishloach Manot and Sophia gets to give out special Purim gift bags to friends, family and their local elderly community. programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: There is an amazing seahorse merry-go-round on the beach, and everyone is having lots of fun going round and round - except Bevel. When he runs away from the ride, Bob finds him, only for Bevel to admit he's nervous about going in circles. Everyone joins in to help him overcome his fear and soon he's sitting on his seahorse, Blue Yah, excited about his first turn on a merry-go-round. But now the key that winds it up has broken, so Bitz needs to work out how to get it started using wind power

programme.title: Olobob Top
programme.desc: Gurdy meets some lovely crabs at the beach, but when they return to the sea Gurdy cannot follow. They make Wiggle Wag who bridges land and sea so Gurdy can be with his friends

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Mr Tumble makes breakfast for Tumble Ted. Meanwhile, Justin and his friends are collecting eggs for their breakfast

programme.title: Maddie, Space and You
programme.desc: Join Maddie as she finds out about the exciting things people do to train to become an astronaut. Maddie explains the different layers of our atmosphere and how astronauts travel through it into space and the special suits they wear to protect them. She tries on a real spacesuit and learns about the types of spacesuits they wear to do different jobs. Maddie discovers astronauts need to be fit, healthy and have good agility. She finds out how they learn how to move in space when they can’t keep their feet on the ground! We discover there is less gravity in space than on Earth and although astronauts look like they are floating, they are free falling.

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug decide to go holiday and use Granny Pug's guide to camping.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The rabbit accidentally brings home Mr MacGregor's cat instead of a cart of Brussels sprouts, and must find a way to lure her into the garden safely

programme.title: Go Jetters

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran are looking for old things to take to the Jumble Sale. JoJo doesn't understand why anybody would want to buy Gran Gran's old things. But she soon discovers that one person's old things can be brand new to somebody else!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Simon and Evie take Waffle for a walk and bump into Evie's friend Anaya and her mum, AJ. An overenthusiastic Waffle drags Simon through the park, soaking him under a sprinkler

programme.title: Andy's Aquatic Adventures
programme.desc: Andy works at Safari World, the biggest and best safari park on the planet, home to animals from all over the globe. He works there with his boss, Mr Hammond and tech wizard Jen, who monitors all the animals from the Aqua Base. Mr Hammond has been up all night in the Jungle Den at Safari World looking through his tape collection of animal sounds he recorded on a trip down the mighty Mississippi river in North America. Andy offers to help so that Mr Hammond can have a nap before the opening of the new alligator zone. Mr Hammond agrees, but he forgets to tell Andy what animal sound he is looking for. Andy puts a tape in the old cassette player, which plays a high-pitched squeaking noise. Thinking it's broken Andy attempts to fix it with a screwdriver and damages it. Suddenly, Mr Hammond returns having remembered what animal sound he wanted Andy to look for. It was the sound of a baby alligator, which Mr Hammond describes as a high-pitched squeaking. Andy realises that the tape he has jus

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear invite babies and their grown-ups to explore gloves through discovery, play, song and story

programme.title: Small Potatoes

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tinpo
programme.desc: When some flowers pop up near Tinpo HQ, there are too many for Logi-Po to water!

programme.title: Treasure Champs

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: An African folk tale explaining why flamingos stand on only one leg

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate uses a special thermal imaging camera to find out how the hot water system in a house works and then visits a reservoir to see where the water comes from. She also visits a factory to see how toothpaste is made

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: JoJo discovers that Gran Gran used to be in a band with Grandad and decides that she wants to learn how to play the drums. Gran Gran teaches JoJo how to play to a beat ,and eventually they form a new band with Jared and Cynthia called JoJo and the Jammers!

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Katie tries to help her parents by delivering the island's mail, but when everything goes wrong Grannie Island has to save the day. Children's drama about a feisty, red-headed girl who lives with her family on the magical Scottish island of Struay. Cherry Campbell and Ann Louise Ross star

programme.desc: The feathered friend wins a trip for two to a rather exciting hotel, and finds that he settles in much quicker than Sarah

programme.desc: Charlie tries to get Lola to go to sleep, but she has too much energy

programme.desc: Mo is always there to help, but Tee is getting bigger and wants to do everything by himself

programme.desc: On cleaning day, all the Clangers are doing a good job dusting. However, they encounter the problem of reaching all the dustweeds they can see on the ceiling of the living cave

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste pick up the case of a man who lost thousands of pounds and his home after falling in love with a woman claiming to be a gold trader. programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Cameras eavesdrop on more tense, confrontational, flirty and often emotional reunions. Geordie Shore star Nathan Henry is desperate to know if ex Tommy was only with him for the fame. Tallia Storm finally reveals the real reason she dumped racing driver Seb Morris six years ago. Will they be able to clear the air and try again, or will the truth leave their exes in exile?

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: The series rounds off with former Apprentice candidate Ryan-Mark Parsons finding out why he was fired from his relationship by Oskars, while actor and broadcaster Mim Shaikh is served up some shocking home truths by his ex, Destiny

programme.title: Tropic Thunder (2008)
programme.desc: A director's epic Vietnam movie is behind schedule and over budget. Taking the advice of the grizzled war veteran on whose life the film is based, he drops the actors into a jungle with the intention of filming them fighting guerrilla-style - but the cast is unaware the surrounding warfare is genuine. Comedy, directed by and starring Ben Stiller, with Jack Black, Steve Coogan and, in an Oscar-nominated role, Robert Downey Jr
