programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the latest meeting of the campaign in Madrid. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is house hunting in Somerset with a couple who have a £675,000 budget to find a rural home that must have annexe potential for their parents

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former world champion boxer Anthony Crolla, Commonwealth silver medallist high jumper Morgan Lake, former British tennis number one Greg Rusedski and Olympic medal-winning swimmer Steve Parry. Sue Barker hosts

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v England (Kick-off 4.45pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the match in the third round of fixtures, held at Principality Stadium. Wales won just one match in last year's championship, but have already bettered that total this time around following narrow victories over Ireland and Scotland, while England bounced back from a shock opening day defeat at home to the Scots with a comfortable triumph over Italy. With analysis from Jeremy Guscott, Martin Johnson and Sam Warburton, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Brian Moore

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In this episode, Rhys and Stephanie, a couple from Tamworth in Staffordshire, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Connie takes it upon herself to get involved in Grace's love life and confronts Leon. Meanwhile, Faith and Lev do everything in their power to keep their marriage together, and Jan is horrified to find her son is in danger, but struggles to accept what she can do to save him. Starring Amanda Mealing and Emily Carey

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton implies that Dryden was embroiled in a wider conspiracy, and the discovery of video recordings made by the witness while in protective custody appears to confirm this. Arnott uncovers evidence that a tracker had been planted on one of the convoy vehicles, and when Denton is given special dispensation to visit her dying mother, the AC-12 officers realise too late how much danger she may be in. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Leeds United v Aston Villa at Elland Road. The Citizens were looking to reinforce their status as title-favourites against a West Ham side that have performed well this term, while the Villans were hoping to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of Leeds at Villa Park in October. Plus, West Bromwich Albion v Brighton & Hove Albion and Newcastle United v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: Invictus (2009)
programme.desc: Following his election as president of South Africa, Nelson Mandela finds himself the leader of a racially and economically divided nation, and seeks a means to unite the people. The country's entry in the 1995 Rugby World Cup provides a perfect opportunity, and he enlists the aid of captain Francois Pienaar in making his team an inspiration to all South Africans. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Morgan Freeman and Matt Damon

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: A service for the second Sunday of Lent

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Fulham (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the Premier League clash at Selhurst Park. Palace have won the last three meetings between the sides, including a 2-1 win at Craven Cottage in October, but Fulham will be determined to end that run of defeats as they are battling to avoid relegation despite some encouraging recent results on their travels

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from Witley Recycling Centre, including a cooker hood and a collection of old jars

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets a couple who have already sold up and are desperate to invest £425,000 in their perfect home in Somerset

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: Pizza Boys
programme.desc: Pizza chefs Jez and Ieuz continue their adventure across Europe in San Sebastian, where they tour the Spanish city's best tabernas sampling pintxos, before heading to Bordeaux

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani is taken on a tour of one of London's cemeteries, as she discovers a trove of treasures right on her doorstep and sets out to crack the mystery of a non-native bird. Adam Henson shows how a super-meal is specially concocted for his animals during the winter months, and Tom Heap investigates the controversial decision that allows UK farmers to use a banned pesticide on their fields

programme.title: Attenborough's Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the many ways animals use colour, with cameras built specifically for this series revealing a world normally invisible to human eyes. The peacock's tail has evolved entirely to impress females and is one of the most extreme colour ornaments in the natural world, while mandrill baboons signal their strength and status with striking red and blue face masks, the brighter the colours, the higher ranking the male

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where items assessed include a rare artefact that survived the attack on Pearl Harbour and an LS Lowry painting. There are also ancient carvings from the Indian subcontinent and an intriguing wooden carving with a mysterious past

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: Protocol will not let Tom Brannick near the island investigation, but driven by his hunger to settle a score from his past, the detective attempts to solve the kidnapping case. With the help of Birdy he is soon in perilous pursuit of a possible connection to the crime, but the question soon arises whether it is Goliath he is hunting, or someone making a false trail. Crime thriller, starring James Nesbitt

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's five Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Manchester United at Stamford Bridge and Sheffield United v Liverpool at Bramall Lane. The Blues and United drew 0-0 when the sides met at Old Trafford in October, but both clubs will have been hoping to claim all three points on this occasion as they looked to boost their hopes of at least finishing in the top four. The Blades have struggled so far this season, but will have drawn hope from Liverpool suffering a number of poor results against bottom-six teams this term. Plus, Crystal Palace v Fulham, Leicester City v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Burnley

programme.title: The Rugby Codebreakers
programme.desc: Carolyn Hitt presents a look at the Welsh rugby players during the 100-year amateur era who broke what was considered a taboo by many to switch codes from union to league. Carolyn travels to Huddersfield where in 1895, members of 22 teams decided to resign from the amateur English Rugby Union, with the professional rugby league being born as a result. She also provides an insight into those from South Wales who found great success after crossing codes in the 1920s and the black players who flourished in the 1970s, including Roy Francis and Clive Sullivan

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v England (Kick-off 4.45pm). Gabby Logan presents all the action from the match in the third round of fixtures, held at Principality Stadium. Wales won just one match in last year's championship, but have already bettered that total this time around following narrow victories over Ireland and Scotland, while England bounced back from a shock opening day defeat at home to the Scots with a comfortable triumph over Italy. With analysis from Jeremy Guscott, Martin Johnson and Sam Warburton, and commentary by Eddie Butler, Jonathan Davies and Brian Moore

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: Game show which sees contestants answer questions that combine knowledge, strategy and luck for the chance to win life-changing prizes. Those taking part won't just require the correct answers, but also the luck of the drop of a single ball, and unflinching trust in one another. In this episode, Rhys and Stephanie, a couple from Tamworth in Staffordshire, face the questions and big decisions, but with a show with so many twists and turns, anything could happen. Hosted by Danny Dyer, with questions voiced by Angela Rippon

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Connie takes it upon herself to get involved in Grace's love life and confronts Leon. Meanwhile, Faith and Lev do everything in their power to keep their marriage together, and Jan is horrified to find her son is in danger, but struggles to accept what she can do to save him. Starring Amanda Mealing and Emily Carey

programme.title: Line of Duty
programme.desc: Denton implies that Dryden was embroiled in a wider conspiracy, and the discovery of video recordings made by the witness while in protective custody appears to confirm this. Arnott uncovers evidence that a tracker had been planted on one of the convoy vehicles, and when Denton is given special dispensation to visit her dying mother, the AC-12 officers realise too late how much danger she may be in. Drama, starring Keeley Hawes

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Leeds United v Aston Villa at Elland Road. The Citizens were looking to reinforce their status as title-favourites against a West Ham side that have performed well this term, while the Villans were hoping to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of Leeds at Villa Park in October. Plus, West Bromwich Albion v Brighton & Hove Albion and Newcastle United v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Jonathan Sutherland presents action from the latest Premiership matches, featuring Celtic v Aberdeen, Hibernian v Motherwell, Kilmarnock v Dundee United and St Mirren v Ross County

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United at Etihad Stadium and Leeds United v Aston Villa at Elland Road. The Citizens were looking to reinforce their status as title-favourites against a West Ham side that have performed well this term, while the Villans were hoping to avenge a 3-0 defeat at the hands of Leeds at Villa Park in October. Plus, West Bromwich Albion v Brighton & Hove Albion and Newcastle United v Wolverhampton Wanderers

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: The Sunday Show
programme.desc: Martin Geissler presents the main issues from the world of politics and beyond, featuring interviews, discussion and analysis on the stories that matter to Scotland

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell hosts the programme that features moral and religious discussions on topical issues

programme.title: Reflections at the Quay
programme.desc: Religious thoughts from Scotland, plus a selection of hymns

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Crystal Palace v Fulham (Kick-off 12.00pm). All the action from the Premier League clash at Selhurst Park. Palace have won the last three meetings between the sides, including a 2-1 win at Craven Cottage in October, but Fulham will be determined to end that run of defeats as they are battling to avoid relegation despite some encouraging recent results on their travels

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore transforms items reclaimed from Witley Recycling Centre, including a cooker hood and a collection of old jars

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman meets a couple who have already sold up and are desperate to invest £425,000 in their perfect home in Somerset

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich descend on gardens in desperate need of a makeover - but must stick to the owners' budgets

programme.title: Seven Worlds, One Planet
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at life in South America, where animals must specialise if they are to carve out a niche. In Patagonia, a puma mother draws on a lifetime's experience to catch prey three times her weight, while rarely seen Andean bears clamber 30 metres into the cloud forest canopy to find elusive fruit. Poison dart frogs use ingenious methods to keep their tadpoles safe, anacondas stalk capuchin monkeys, and swifts make death-defying flights through Igauzu Falls on the border between Argentina and Brazil

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Anita Rani is taken on a tour of one of London's cemeteries, as she discovers a trove of treasures right on her doorstep and sets out to crack the mystery of a non-native bird. Adam Henson shows how a super-meal is specially concocted for his animals during the winter months, and Tom Heap investigates the controversial decision that allows UK farmers to use a banned pesticide on their fields

programme.title: Attenborough's Life in Colour
programme.desc: David Attenborough explores the many ways animals use colour, with cameras built specifically for this series revealing a world normally invisible to human eyes. The peacock's tail has evolved entirely to impress females and is one of the most extreme colour ornaments in the natural world, while mandrill baboons signal their strength and status with striking red and blue face masks, the brighter the colours, the higher ranking the male

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team are at Stonor Park in Oxfordshire, where items assessed include a rare artefact that survived the attack on Pearl Harbour and an LS Lowry painting. There are also ancient carvings from the Indian subcontinent and an intriguing wooden carving with a mysterious past

programme.title: Bloodlands
programme.desc: Protocol will not let Tom Brannick near the island investigation, but driven by his hunger to settle a score from his past, the detective attempts to solve the kidnapping case. With the help of Birdy he is soon in perilous pursuit of a possible connection to the crime, but the question soon arises whether it is Goliath he is hunting, or someone making a false trail. Crime thriller, starring James Nesbitt

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents action from today's five Premier League fixtures, including Chelsea v Manchester United at Stamford Bridge and Sheffield United v Liverpool at Bramall Lane. The Blues and United drew 0-0 when the sides met at Old Trafford in October, but both clubs will have been hoping to claim all three points on this occasion as they looked to boost their hopes of at least finishing in the top four. The Blades have struggled so far this season, but will have drawn hope from Liverpool suffering a number of poor results against bottom-six teams this term. Plus, Crystal Palace v Fulham, Leicester City v Arsenal and Tottenham Hotspur v Burnley

programme.title: The Rugby Codebreakers
programme.desc: Carolyn Hitt presents a look at the Welsh rugby players during the 100-year amateur era who broke what was considered a taboo by many to switch codes from union to league. Carolyn travels to Huddersfield where in 1895, members of 22 teams decided to resign from the amateur English Rugby Union, with the professional rugby league being born as a result. She also provides an insight into those from South Wales who found great success after crossing codes in the 1920s and the black players who flourished in the 1970s, including Roy Francis and Clive Sullivan

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a Victorian terrace in York, a plot of land in Rainham, Kent and a flood-damaged Cumbrian property

programme.title: For Love or Money
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Ashley John-Baptiste pick up the case of a man who lost thousands of pounds and his home after falling in love with a woman claiming to be a gold trader. The story takes a bizarre twist as the mysterious woman known as Maryanne starts making financial demands of them too

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Kent Police officers risk their lives to rescue an individual trapped on a cliff face and West Midlands Police track down a man in possession of a gun

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams find potentially profitable items at the East of England showground in Peterborough, while presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp attempts to get to the bottom of an ancient mystery

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: A simple missing person's case takes a dramatic turn as Frank and Lu find themselves in the middle of a dispute over the opening of a new Shakespeare Centre where it's clear that no one is what they seem. Simon Williams and Josie Lawrence guest star

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King head off into the heart of traditional Japan to find out about the historic roots of the nation's cuisine. Dave puts his Strictly fancy footwork into action by kneading noodle dough the artisan way, before the duo then take an awe-inspiring journey to see Mount Fuji, spend a night at a Buddhist temple and are entertained by one of Kyoto's geisha complete with traditional drinking games

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin embarks on a property hunting mission in Oxfordshire with a young couple who are both medical professionals and have a sizable £1.5million budget to find a country home for their growing family. Away from potential properties, Jonnie braves the Oxfordshire waters during an exhilarating attempt at punt racing

programme.title: Jay and Dom's Home Fix
programme.desc: Jay Blades makes a useful magnetic tool organiser, while Dom Chinea creates a planter complete with decorative house numbers. For the big build the pair vie to make the most coveted of household features - the kitchen island. From the archive, Rachel de Thame digs up an unwanted garden tree and Gay Search reveals a clever way to turn an unappealing side return into an attractive outdoor space

programme.title: The Bidding Room
programme.desc: Carl from Stoke brings in a key that turns out to be not quite what it appears. Alice, from Wales, tries to sell a moulded model advertisement for men's underwear from the early 20th century. Naz from Halifax presents a harmonica that she wants to sell so she and her young son can have a holiday, and Sharon from Hellsby tries to interest the dealers in a Victorian silver belt. Nigel Havers presents

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and a co-host round off the working week with the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by former world champion boxer Anthony Crolla, Commonwealth silver medallist high jumper Morgan Lake, former British tennis number one Greg Rusedski and Olympic medal-winning swimmer Steve Parry. Sue Barker hosts

programme.title: Gavin & Stacey
programme.desc: After a day spent house-hunting, the newlyweds take up an invitation from Nessa and Smithy to relax with a trip to the bowling alley. However, when tensions between Gavin and Stacey reach a head at the end of the night, their two friends begin to regret making the offer

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Stacey is fuming that Lily has ordered glam holiday stuff on her credit card and vows to make Ruby pay for it. Dotty pitches her idea to a distracted Ruby, who eventually agrees to allow Dotty and Tiff to host tables. Lola is fed up about Isaac, Kim offers to match Mila next, and Tiff and Keegan celebrate their new room at Sonia's

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Bank Balance
programme.desc: Contestants Kate and Jonathan and brothers Kurt and Eren answer a series of questions in order to place different sized stacks of gold bars on a volatile Balance Board and the more bars they build up, the more they can win to a jackpot of £100,000. However, the more bars that accumulate, the greater the chance they will fall down requiring players to demonstrate both knowledge and nerve to triumph in a game show that's as unpredictable in nature as its host

programme.title: The Paddy Raff Show
programme.desc: More sketches featuring the popular stand-up comedian, showcasing his unique and quirky take on modern life. This episode comprises a compilation of the biggest belly-laughs from the series, the 2020 Christmas special and the original pilot show

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host is joined by Hollywood star Kate Winslet, who talks about her role in romantic Victorian drama Ammonite. Accomplished actor Stanley Tucci discusses his role as a writer suffering dementia in Supernova, Pirates of the Caribbean's Orlando Bloom chats about powerful revenge drama Retaliation, while rising British star Kingsley Ben-Adir talks about his part in sci-fi anthology Soulmates. Plus, Silk City featuring Ellie Goulding perform New Love

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Cameras eavesdrop on more tense, confrontational, flirty and often emotional reunions. Geordie Shore star Nathan Henry is desperate to know if ex Tommy was only with him for the fame. Tallia Storm finally reveals the real reason she dumped racing driver Seb Morris six years ago. Will they be able to clear the air and try again, or will the truth leave their exes in exile?

programme.title: Tropic Thunder (2008)
programme.desc: A director's epic Vietnam movie is behind schedule and over budget. Taking the advice of the grizzled war veteran on whose life the film is based, he drops the actors into a jungle with the intention of filming them fighting guerrilla-style - but the cast is unaware the surrounding warfare is genuine. Comedy, directed by and starring Ben Stiller, with Jack Black, Steve Coogan and, in an Oscar-nominated role, Robert Downey Jr

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Naga Munchetty presents a round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: This week Saturday Kitchen is hosted by Matt Tebbutt and he's joined by chefs Dr Rupy Aujla and Georgina Hayden, plus special guest Emily Atack. Drinks expert Helen McGinn joins via video link and picks the wines to go with the studio dishes

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornwall
programme.desc: As the chef's Cornish odyssey nears its end, he takes a break in the city of Truro, where he discovers an unusual ghost story and tours the magnificent cathedral. He also meets a family passionate about offal, who prepare Rick a meal of pickled ox tongue and beef heart tacos, and in the county's most northerly parish he tells the story of an eccentric vicar who would retrieve the bodies from nearby shipwrecks and bury them in his church graveyard

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Manchester City v West Ham United, Leicester City v Arsenal and Chelsea v Manchester United

programme.title: Community Life
programme.desc: An appeal for support from the Air Ambulance, a trip with Fermanagh Community Transport, and the Bangor food bank helping out in the pandemic

programme.title: Athletics
programme.desc: Action from the latest meeting of the campaign in Madrid. With commentary by Steve Cram and Andrew Cotter

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Jay Blades is at Altrincham Recycling Centre in Greater Manchester, where expert Duncan McKean and furniture Chloe Kempster transform an old radiogram and a chest of drawers

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden in Northampton that will allow two children with limited mobility access to the outdoor space

programme.title: Invictus (2009)
programme.desc: Following his election as president of South Africa, Nelson Mandela finds himself the leader of a racially and economically divided nation, and seeks a means to unite the people. The country's entry in the 1995 Rugby World Cup provides a perfect opportunity, and he enlists the aid of captain Francois Pienaar in making his team an inspiration to all South Africans. Clint Eastwood's fact-based drama, starring Morgan Freeman and Matt Damon

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: Glasgow City Council has a target to achieve carbon neutrality by 2030. The council plans to make changes to public transport, food systems and heating. Annabel Maclennan investigates the biggest challenges faced by the council and looks at what needs to be done in order to meet this target. There is also a look at how the city of Copenhagen, in Denmark, hopes to achieve carbon neutrality even earlier - by 2025

programme.title: Raiola: The Super-Agent Speaks Out
programme.desc: He's the man behind some of football's biggest transfers with links all around the globe. 'Super-agent' Mino Raiola has a client list that includes Manchester United's Paul Pogba and AC Milan's Zlatan Ibrahamovic. But with the sport facing an unprecedented financial crisis because of the coronavirus pandemic, there is mounting scrutiny on agents, and the vast sums they can earn from transfers. In a rare interview, one of the game's most controversial and influential figures has been speaking to the BBC Sports Editor Dan Roan about the issues affecting football and the important role that agents play.

programme.title: Eòrpa
programme.desc: Glasgow City Council has a target to achieve carbon neutrality by 2030. The council plans to make changes to public transport, food systems and heating. Annabel Maclennan investigates the biggest challenges faced by the council and looks at what needs to be done in order to meet this target. There is also a look at how the city of Copenhagen, in Denmark, hopes to achieve carbon neutrality even earlier - by 2025

programme.title: An Taigh-Fuine/The Bakery
programme.desc: People from North Uist are out in their droves for the cattle show and where there is a crowd, there will be a need for food. The bakery staff are never idle, with lots of preparation for children in South Uist celebrating their First Holy Communion and for a special event taking place in Benbecula

programme.title: Tearlach Ceannadach/Charles Kennedy: A Good Man Speaking

programme.title: Lorraine

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five university friends compete in the game show, taking it in turns to find the answers to top 10 lists, before trying to score a perfect 10 in the final round. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Charlotte, Ben, Keith and Melanie work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There is a Dingle court decider, Gabby has some news, Laurel worries, and Vinny wants to help

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Having witnessed an assault, Sam flees with Simon's bags of cocaine. Tim reveals to Sally that according to Geoff's will, he now owns half of No 6 and a quarter of Speed Daal. Elsewhere, Gail and Eileen's bickering leaves George fuming, while Dev and Mary go overboard in trying to impress Nina

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The successful businesswoman behind an empire of beauty salons throws a lavish party on board a boat on the River Tyne - only for her sister to be found dead in the water. Vera's investigation uncovers the possibility that the victim may have been in an abusive relationship - but may also have been about to bring a terrible secret to light that threatened her entire family

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gives six new contestants the chance to take their place in the hot seat, with lifelines, including Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: While Cole gets into the holiday spirit for his first Christmas with Maya, Murtaugh hopes he can convince RJ to come back home from Costa Rica for the holidays. At the precinct, Cole and Murtaugh are saddled with a complicated burglary and home invasion case. Meanwhile, Avery's holidays are a bit blue, and Bailey is still trying to figure out her new partner. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The Spuds don't care about a carbon footprint, since George has come home with a haul of free electronics that they all want to test out.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave wants to learn to play the drums at school, but is given the recorder. With a talent show around the corner, Dave gets practising.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur

programme.title: Mission Employable
programme.desc: Agent Danny finds out what it takes to man the controls of a cross-country locomotive when he spends the day as a train driver

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless halfwit finds a treasure map in a dusty corner of his attic, but the road to riches proves a rocky one

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless halfwit finds himself out on the street after his landlady orders him to leave. Near to tears, he is desperate for the comforts of home again, but fortunately a scarecrow and a pair of false teeth soon come to his rescue. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike realises there's nothing better to impress your crush with than a little magic trick, but things soon get out of hand.

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics. Bumblebee moves in, but finds it hard to adjust

programme.title: Kung Fu Panda: The Paws of Destiny
programme.desc: Po and the Kids manage to escape the pursuing police in a giant sea battle to make it to Volcano Island and get some answers, and Empress Xiao begins learning how to defend herself.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Dave is practising for Grimsby's Grimmest Face competition and finds that the wind changing direction really can cause the face to stick...

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: JJames is joined in the kitchen by guests Radio One star Mollie King and chefs Ravneet Gill and Tony Tobin. He demonstrates recipes for prawn jalfrezi, bolognese and chocolate fudge cake, while Ravneet and Tony whip up some dishes of their own

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef pays culinary homage to his mother's cooking by preparing some childhood favourites, including vegetable soup three ways and an assiette of salads made with produce fresh from his gardens. He is joined by Nathan Outlaw, who serves up a dish of fish and chips with mushy peas

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef ends his coast-to-coast exploration of the US with a visit to one of his all-time favourite foodie hotspots - New York City. After a bite of pizza at a popular pizzeria in Greenwich Village, James cooks on one of New York's famous piers. He also tries the city's fine dining ahead of reminiscing on his journey of over 3,000 miles

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Italy v Ireland (Kick-off 2.15pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the match in the third round of fixtures, held at Stadio Olimpico. Ireland narrowly lost their opening two matches in Wales and away to France, but will be expecting to get a victory on the board against the Italians, who have lost their last 29 contests in the championship. With analysis from Jonny Wilkinson, Brian O'Driscoll, Gareth Thomas and Rory Best, and commentary from Miles Harrison, Geordan Murphy and Ben Kay

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as The Last Leg presenter Alex Brooker, Emmerdale actor Chris Bisson and reality star Joey Essex take part in the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping counters down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - with the highest scorer playing for the £20,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity special of the classic game show, with contestants Mo Gilligan, Naga Munchetty and Joanna Page all trying to say what they see, as they guess well-known phrases for the chance to win a £50,000 jackpot for their chosen charity

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The hosts are joined by Andi Peters for another helping of Saturday Night Takeaway, bursting with big surprises, live games and musical performances, with Gordon Ramsay in the Star Guest announcer booth. The Pandas Bam and Boo make a return to the show after their popular debut in the previous series, and this time they are trying to make their escape from a teddy bear's picnic display in Hamleys, and need the help of some young customers in making a fast getaway. Ant and Dec embark on a brand-new adventure in clones-gone-rogue comedy serial Double Trouble. Plus, the Takeaway Rainbow will alight somewhere in the UK and another lucky player gets the chance to Win The Ads

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the second of this year's Battle Rounds in the singing contest. Coaches will.i.am, Tom Jones, Olly Murs and Anne-Marie all return to their infamous red chairs as the acts they have chosen from the blind auditions go head to head and fight for their place on their coach's team. The singers that impress their coach the most remain in the competition with a chance of winning a recording contract. Will those that aren't so lucky be stolen by another coach or will they be going home?

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show: Special Guests
programme.desc: A look back at some of the most memorable moments from recent series of the talk show. Amongst the guests featured are Celine Dion, David Beckham, Tina Turner, Samuel L Jackson, Lindsey Lohan, Paul McCartney, Amy Schumer, Michael Douglas, Jennifer Hudson, Kit Harrington and Samson Kayo

programme.title: The World Is Not Enough (1999)
programme.desc: James Bond is assigned to protect the heiress of an oil dynasty after her father is killed in a bombing at MI6 headquarters. The attack is the work of an infamous terrorist who is unable to feel pain after surviving a gunshot to his head, and who now intends to destroy the world's supply of oil. Spy adventure, starring Pierce Brosnan, Robert Carlyle, Sophie Marceau and Robbie Coltrane

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers compete in a series of challenges to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave and Fuzzypeg the dog get dirty playing at the local dump, so dirty in fact that Fuzzypeg picks up an extremely grumpy flea.

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: Dave's first plane journey doesn't go to plan when the air pressure and the need to go to the toilet makes his head swell.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur

programme.title: Mission Employable
programme.desc: Agent Kym takes on the challenges of a farmhand and discovers that there are lots of jobs to do there. For her first job of the day, she has to feed the calves

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: An old photo album causes Bean to reminisce about the happy holidays he spent at Scout camp as a boy - and prompts him to buy a tent in a bid to recapture his childhood

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless half-wit is furious when a child blasts his model aeroplane out of the sky, and soon plots revenge _ leading to an all-out battle for airspace over London. Animated comedy, with the voices of Rowan Atkinson, Jon Glover and Sally Grace

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike's special date with Iris is interrupted when the raccoons get fleas and head over to Mike's to find something to relieve their itchiness.

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: While investigating the appearance of strange graffiti at sites all over Metropolis, the girls run across a second group of heroes.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: The teenage superheroes fight to save the world while getting up to mischievous antics

programme.title: Don't Unleash the Beast
programme.desc: An immersive game show, set in the mythological underground world of Halian, where a team of three intrepid treasure seekers face a series of obstacles in their quest to retrieve ancient artefacts for The Professor, an archaeological expert in all things Halian. There's just one tiny snag - a ferocious and foul Beast is guarding the treasure. One wrong move and it could be unleashed!

programme.title: The Rubbish World of Dave Spud
programme.desc: The misadventures of underdog Dave Spud and his family. Gran has been particularly naughty lately and is sentenced to a stay in an old people's home, but the home is the stuff of nightmares for Gran, and it's soon up to the Spuds to break her free.

programme.title: Cornwall and Devon Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: As the series draws to a close, the presenter walks the secluded coves along the Helford River in a relaxing three-mile loop from Helford Village to Frenchman's Creek and back. Whilst in the area, Julia goes oyster fishing and tries her hand at stand-up paddle boarding, before finishing with a visit to the Cornish Seal Sanctuary

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan welcomes Tom Chambers and Cerys Matthews to Manor Farm, while gardener David Domoney gets fruity and Alan celebrates St David's Day with some of Wales' finest tipples

programme.title: Simply Raymond Blanc
programme.desc: The chef pays culinary homage to his mother's cooking by preparing some childhood favourites, including vegetable soup three ways and an assiette of salads made with produce fresh from his gardens. He is joined by Nathan Outlaw, who serves up a dish of fish and chips with mushy peas

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team of experts, Katie Rushworth, Frances Tophill and David Domoney, creates a modern family garden in Hucknall near Nottingham. The project is a surprise for a landscape gardener who was diagnosed with Alzheimer's at the age of just 32, making him one of the youngest people in the country to develop the condition and leaving him physically unable to complete the garden personally. Alan also provides viewers with some tips on sensory planting and creating home-made play equipment for children

programme.title: The Lost World: Jurassic Park (1997)
programme.desc: Scientist Dr Ian Malcolm reluctantly joins a research team on the island where his employer first designed genetically engineered dinosaurs. Meanwhile, a big-game hunter takes the chance to bag the biggest prize of all - a Tyrannosaurus rex. Steven Spielberg's dinosaur adventure sequel, starring Jeff Goldblum, Julianne Moore, Pete Postlethwaite and Richard Attenborough

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a special Emmerdale episode of the quiz show, as celebrity contestants Anthony Quinlan, Louisa Clein, James Moore and Dominic Brunt pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby welcome the remaining celebrities and their professional partners as they take on Movie Week. Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel, alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman. Plus, there is a performance from some of the professional skaters

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Return of the drama about two chalk-and-cheese police partners solving crimes on the streets of Bath. In the first episode, five friends take a hot-air balloon trip but only four come back down alive, leaving McDonald and Dodds to untangle a complex web of lies spanning 30 years. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances from Rob Brydon, Rupert Graves, Martin Kemp and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry welcomes John Barnes and Chris Kamara, where they take to the water to see what the area has to offer from the sea. Harry also challenges John and Chris to a powerboat showdown on the Solent, before they sample a local delicacy at Harry's favourite chip shop

programme.title: Million Pound Pawn
programme.desc: Documentary series about pawnbrokers and their customers, and the impact the economic crisis has had on the trade. In the first episode, a businessman tries to sell his prized Lamborghini to veteran pawnbroker Ray, who knows he can only afford to take the car if he can find a buyer. A woman tries to sell a watch that could be valuable to a life-changing amount - but could also be a fake. A former 1980s pop star fallen on hard times visits a pawnbroker to raise some much-needed funds

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Ugo Monye presents action from the third round of fixtures, featuring France v Scotland at Stade de France, Wales v England at Principality Stadium and Italy v Ireland at Stadio Olimpico. With analysis from Jim Hamilton and Nolli Waterman

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Snetterton featuring round 19 of the Ginetta Junior Championship and round 23 of the British F4 Championship

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Tipping Point

The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Five university friends compete in the game show, taking it in turns to find the answers to top 10 lists, before trying to score a perfect 10 in the final round. Hosted by Warwick Davis

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Charlotte, Ben, Keith and Melanie work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: There is a Dingle court decider, Gabby has some news, Laurel worries, and Vinny wants to help

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Having witnessed an assault, Sam flees with Simon's bags of cocaine. Tim reveals to Sally that according to Geoff's will, he now owns half of No 6 and a quarter of Speed Daal. Elsewhere, Gail and Eileen's bickering leaves George fuming, while Dev and Mary go overboard in trying to impress Nina

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The successful businesswoman behind an empire of beauty salons throws a lavish party on board a boat on the River Tyne - only for her sister to be found dead in the water. Vera's investigation uncovers the possibility that the victim may have been in an abusive relationship - but may also have been about to bring a terrible secret to light that threatened her entire family

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson gives six new contestants the chance to take their place in the hot seat, with lifelines, including Phone a Friend and 50-50 available to them if they get stuck

programme.title: FYI Extra
programme.desc: Short bulletin rounding up the day's showbusiness stories

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: While Cole gets into the holiday spirit for his first Christmas with Maya, Murtaugh hopes he can convince RJ to come back home from Costa Rica for the holidays. At the precinct, Cole and Murtaugh are saddled with a complicated burglary and home invasion case. Meanwhile, Avery's holidays are a bit blue, and Bailey is still trying to figure out her new partner. Action drama, starring Damon Wayans and Seann William Scott

programme.title: The Lost World: Jurassic Park (1997)
programme.desc: Scientist Dr Ian Malcolm reluctantly joins a research team on the island where his employer first designed genetically engineered dinosaurs. Meanwhile, a big-game hunter takes the chance to bag the biggest prize of all - a Tyrannosaurus rex. Steven Spielberg's dinosaur adventure sequel, starring Jeff Goldblum, Julianne Moore, Pete Postlethwaite and Richard Attenborough

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a special Emmerdale episode of the quiz show, as celebrity contestants Anthony Quinlan, Louisa Clein, James Moore and Dominic Brunt pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby welcome the remaining celebrities and their professional partners as they take on Movie Week. Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel, alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman. Plus, there is a performance from some of the professional skaters

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: Return of the drama about two chalk-and-cheese police partners solving crimes on the streets of Bath. In the first episode, five friends take a hot-air balloon trip but only four come back down alive, leaving McDonald and Dodds to untangle a complex web of lies spanning 30 years. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia star, with guest appearances from Rob Brydon, Rupert Graves, Martin Kemp and Patsy Kensit

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Harry welcomes John Barnes and Chris Kamara, where they take to the water to see what the area has to offer from the sea. Harry also challenges John and Chris to a powerboat showdown on the Solent, before they sample a local delicacy at Harry's favourite chip shop

programme.title: Million Pound Pawn
programme.desc: Documentary series about pawnbrokers and their customers, and the impact the economic crisis has had on the trade. In the first episode, a businessman tries to sell his prized Lamborghini to veteran pawnbroker Ray, who knows he can only afford to take the car if he can find a buyer. A woman tries to sell a watch that could be valuable to a life-changing amount - but could also be a fake. A former 1980s pop star fallen on hard times visits a pawnbroker to raise some much-needed funds

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: Ugo Monye presents action from the third round of fixtures, featuring France v Scotland at Stade de France, Wales v England at Principality Stadium and Italy v Ireland at Stadio Olimpico. With analysis from Jim Hamilton and Nolli Waterman

programme.desc: Fiona tells her family about how she slept with Jim. Liz is distraught as no one will tell her why Jim tried to stop the wedding. Maud accepts Fred's offer for the shop

programme.desc: Liz insults Fiona for leading on Jim and Steve. Steve tells Jim he no longer wants to work with him. Jon is surprised when Deirdre asks him to marry her

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Midsomer Malham resident is accused of battering her best friend to death, but Barnaby cannot help feeling the evidence against her does not add up. With John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: New neighbours look set to disturb the tranquillity of Portwenn, upsetting the locals with their unconventional lifestyle and even managing to gatecrash an intimate dinner between Martin and Louisa. Meanwhile, a car vandal strikes the village - but agoraphobic village copper PC Penhale is too scared to leave the station and investigate. Martin Clunes stars

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa faces off with love rival Carrie in a battle for Martin's affections, and romance is in the air for Aunt Joan, who gains an admirer in the form of Edward. Elsewhere, Al makes a surprise reappearance and PC Penhale celebrates his birthday. Martin Clunes stars, with Joe Absolom and Louise Delamere

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Rachel finds out about an affair Nick once had with a juror in the middle of a trial - a revelation that could end his career. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin fails his catering exams and decides to apply for a job in Southampton. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Sarah is pleased when Lucas calls at Underworld to do business, but Carla masks her discomfort. Steve is put out to discover that Eileen's arranged for Tim to drive the hearse at Ted's funeral. Simon shows off to Jacob, telling him about his criminal past as a gang member

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the death of a local aristocrat who seemingly died of a coronary - shortly after declaring he had an important announcement to make. After the peer's will disappears and his lawyer is killed in an arson attack, it is clear sinister forces are at work. However, the prime suspects all appear to have cast-iron alibis. Guest starring Joss Ackland and Sian Phillips, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. A phone number on the dead man's blotter sends the duo to the seaside in search of his latest targets - whose boat trip could conceal a motive for the killing. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Worthy amateur choir is thrown into panic when baritone Connor Simpson is killed on the eve of the prestigious regional Four Choirs competition. A birdwatcher is also murdered shortly after, and DCI Barnaby and DC Jones uncover bitter rivalry and tense marital relationships as they try to establish if there is a connection between the two cases. Peter Capaldi guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The chalk-and-cheese detective duo are called to a private clinic where a wealthy patient has been killed. However, their investigation is made all the more difficult by the complex relationships of the victim's former fellow residents. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia take the lead roles in the second of two mystery dramas, with an all-star supporting cast including Caroline Catz, Hugh Dennis, Michele Dotrice, Freddie Fox, Suzanne Packer and Joanna Scanlan

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse meets his intellectual match when a serial killer haunting the streets of Oxford keeps himself one step ahead of the law and leaves cryptic messages to goad the police. At first glance, it seems to be the work of a deranged lunatic, but the elaborate staging of the crimes suggests method in the madness - and a macabre sharing of the sleuth's passion for opera. Starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Crime drama following the personal and professional lives of two detectives working for a Manchester police unit that specialises in murder cases. DC Janet Scott (Lesley Sharp) and DC Rachel Bailey (Suranne Jones) investigate the case of a pregnant woman who was killed in her bedroom. Meanwhile, Rachel's love life takes a turn for the worse and Janet gets a call that reawakens a past tragedy. Also starring Rupert Graves, Amelia Bullmore and Nicholas Gleaves

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet reopens the investigation into the murder of childhood friend Veronica, Rachel receives news that makes her personal life even more complicated, and a teenage murderer is successfully charged, but horrific events start to unravel following his arrest. Crime drama, guest starring Sally Lindsay, with Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Ant & Dec's DNA Journey
programme.desc: Anthony McPartlin and Declan Donnelly may think they know who they are, but in this two-part documentary, the popular presenters uncover their surprising and emotional family histories, using a combination of DNA and genealogy. The shock revelations include military men in both of their families - one a medal-winning war hero, the other a deserter. And as well as delving into the past, Ant and Dec also get to meet DNA relatives they never knew existed

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: The staff prepare to let their hair down at their annual gala, where they celebrate their most profitable year and busiest season. It's also where the prestigious Front of House star award will be announced - and concierge Dean, who has been nominated, practises his dance moves in between dealing with guests. Meanwhile, Savoy Grill director Thierry prepares for another visit from owner Gordon Ramsay and quality executive Debra does her daily rounds to check things are in perfect condition. But there is a crisis looming as news of Covid-19 reaches the hotel

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The murder of a babysitter throws up a number of questions for Lewis and Hathaway - was she the intended victim or was the killer targeting her employers? Was the crime calculated or opportunistic? And why was she elaborately tied up after being murdered? As the detectives dig for clues, they are led into a world of suburban swinging and fetish photography, far removed from the Oxford they know. Gary Kemp (The Krays), Lucy Cohu (The Awakening), Ciaran McMenamin (Primeval) and Georgia Taylor (Casualty) guest star

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel discovers her ex-partner is the defending barrister for Georgios Stelikos, a man on trial for rape and murder, and she is horrified when the accused escapes conviction. When Stelikos is later found dead, the prime suspect is Hannah Conway, who made public death threats against him after giving evidence for the prosecution in court. Meanwhile, Janet tries to support her colleague following devastating news. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Three weeks after reporting her husband missing, adult-film star Vicky Birkinshaw is arrested on suspicion of his murder. Rachel faces the task of going through the suspect's home-made pornography collection in search of clues, but the case takes a dark twist when Vicky's daughter turns up with her much older boyfriend. Meanwhile, Janet's affair with a colleague is exposed. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alan storms out of the church and goes for Jim. The Middletons and Maxine try to comfort Fiona. Andy turns on Steve, saying he is as much to blame for ruining the wedding

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Mandy tells Kelly that she accepts she and Paddy are finished forever. Butch acts strangely now that he is a married man. Sarah and Viv head off to Bolton Abbey with the school

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak does not realise the trouble he has caused. Mandy is still unaware that Butch is in love with her. Rachel demands to know where she stands with Graham

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The arrival of a traumatised and wounded stranger in Aidensfield coincides with the discovery of a woman's body on the moors, and sets off a treacherous chain of events that leaves one long-time resident's life hanging in the balance. Nicholas Farrell and Clare Holman guest star in the last-ever episode of the long-running police drama

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean is delighted when Sid actually agrees with her for once, but their plans for the day end in disaster. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Chrissy discovers she has an admirer, while Mr Roper thinks his wife is seeing someone else. Comedy series, with Richard O'Sullivan and Brian Murphy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a victim of fraud is found murdered on a golf course - but his inquiries are complicated by the discovery of a tramp's mutilated body nearby. To make matters worse, beautiful singer Bella Duveen is also around, seemingly intent on helping herself to a grim souvenir. David Suchet stars, with Hugh Fraser, Bill Moody and Jacinta Mulcahy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A London policeman and his doctor wife encounter hostility and suspicion when they move to a close-knit Yorkshire community, but feuding mods and rockers, a mysterious prowler and a budgie-killer mean the couple soon find themselves in the thick of village life. First-ever episode of the popular Sixties-set drama, starring Nick Berry, Niamh Cusack, Derek Fowlds and Bill Maynard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel is not satisfied with Graham's explanation. Laura threatens Chris with the sack. Marlon invites himself to go to London with Kathy. Kim refuses to give Steve any money

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The villagers prepare for their weekend in London. Rachel and Graham make their romance public. Steve incriminates Kim. Tricia tries to come clean to Terry

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiona tells her family about how she slept with Jim. Liz is distraught as no one will tell her why Jim tried to stop the wedding. Maud accepts Fred's offer for the shop

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Liz insults Fiona for leading on Jim and Steve. Steve tells Jim he no longer wants to work with him. Jon is surprised when Deirdre asks him to marry her

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A Midsomer Malham resident is accused of battering her best friend to death, but Barnaby cannot help feeling the evidence against her does not add up. The detective enlists the help of a pensioner, who also happens to be one of the key witnesses, to find out what really happened on the night of the murder - and realises the elderly lady's life could be at risk. With John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Thelma Barlow

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth heads to Lake Windermere for a holiday with faithful assistant Hastings, only to find his crime-solving skills are once again needed to investigate a murder. The only witness to the crime is a dog, leaving Poirot with a difficult task to find the killer. David Suchet stars, with Kate Buffery, Patrick Ryecart and Julia St John

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: New neighbours look set to disturb the tranquillity of Portwenn, upsetting the locals with their unconventional lifestyle and even managing to gatecrash an intimate dinner between Martin and Louisa. Meanwhile, a car vandal strikes the village - but agoraphobic village copper PC Penhale is too scared to leave the station and investigate. Martin Clunes stars

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa faces off with love rival Carrie in a battle for Martin's affections, and romance is in the air for Aunt Joan, who gains an admirer in the form of Edward. Elsewhere, Al makes a surprise reappearance and PC Penhale celebrates his birthday. Martin Clunes stars, with Joe Absolom and Louise Delamere

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet moves a step closer to tracking down Veronica's killer while investigating a recent murder that bears similarities to the cold case. Rachel finds out about an affair Nick once had with a juror in the middle of a trial - a revelation that could end his career. Detective drama, starring Lesley Sharp, Suranne Jones and Rupert Graves

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Pawnbroker Peter Lawson is shot dead in an armed robbery at his premises and Frost's investigations unearth a tragic family secret. He is convinced Lawson was killed by one of his children in revenge for years of systematic sexual abuse - but the truth turns out to be even more chilling. David Jason stars, with Diane Keen, Isla Blair, Matt Bardock, Anthony Bate, Mark Lambert and Edward Purver

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin and Chrissy go babysitting, but the baby will not stop crying, and the drinks cupboard is locked. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan and Paula Wilcox

programme.title: Man About the House
programme.desc: Robin fails his catering exams and decides to apply for a job in Southampton. Comedy, starring Richard O'Sullivan

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Omnibus. Sarah is pleased when Lucas calls at Underworld to do business, but Carla masks her discomfort. Steve is put out to discover that Eileen's arranged for Tim to drive the hearse at Ted's funeral. Simon shows off to Jacob, telling him about his criminal past as a gang member

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Jones investigate the death of a local aristocrat who seemingly died of a coronary - shortly after declaring he had an important announcement to make. After the peer's will disappears and his lawyer is killed in an arson attack, it is clear sinister forces are at work. However, the prime suspects all appear to have cast-iron alibis. Guest starring Joss Ackland and Sian Phillips, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: While investigating the shooting of a council clerk, Barnaby and Jones discover he was an expert blackmailer. A phone number on the dead man's blotter sends the duo to the seaside in search of his latest targets - whose boat trip could conceal a motive for the killing. Meanwhile, Joyce is convinced a local farmer knows more than he is telling. Julia McKenzie and Grant Masters guest star, with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The Midsomer Worthy amateur choir is thrown into panic when baritone Connor Simpson is killed on the eve of the prestigious regional Four Choirs competition. A birdwatcher is also murdered shortly after, and DCI Barnaby and DC Jones uncover bitter rivalry and tense marital relationships as they try to establish if there is a connection between the two cases. Peter Capaldi guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall

programme.title: McDonald & Dodds
programme.desc: The chalk-and-cheese detective duo are called to a private clinic where a wealthy patient has been killed. However, their investigation is made all the more difficult by the complex relationships of the victim's former fellow residents. Jason Watkins and Tala Gouveia take the lead roles in the second of two mystery dramas, with an all-star supporting cast including Caroline Catz, Hugh Dennis, Michele Dotrice, Freddie Fox, Suzanne Packer and Joanna Scanlan

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: Morse meets his intellectual match when a serial killer haunting the streets of Oxford keeps himself one step ahead of the law and leaves cryptic messages to goad the police. At first glance, it seems to be the work of a deranged lunatic, but the elaborate staging of the crimes suggests method in the madness - and a macabre sharing of the sleuth's passion for opera. Starring Shaun Evans, with Roger Allam, Anton Lesser, Sean Rigby and James Bradshaw

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Crime drama following the personal and professional lives of two detectives working for a Manchester police unit that specialises in murder cases. DC Janet Scott (Lesley Sharp) and DC Rachel Bailey (Suranne Jones) investigate the case of a pregnant woman who was killed in her bedroom. Meanwhile, Rachel's love life takes a turn for the worse and Janet gets a call that reawakens a past tragedy. Also starring Rupert Graves, Amelia Bullmore and Nicholas Gleaves

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet reopens the investigation into the murder of childhood friend Veronica, Rachel receives news that makes her personal life even more complicated, and a teenage murderer is successfully charged, but horrific events start to unravel following his arrest. Crime drama, guest starring Sally Lindsay, with Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones
