programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Joyce Barnaby swerves off the road into a ditch, convinced she has hit someone, but nobody is found injured and Tom thinks she has imagined it. However, when a local historian discovers the body of a librarian in a nearby cemetery, Joyce blames herself for the man's death, leading her husband into a spooky investigation to find the real killer on the loose in the haunted village of March Magna. Guest starring Danny Webb (The Bill), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Ant & Dec's DNA Journey
programme.desc: Anthony McPartlin and Declan Donnelly may think they know who they are, but in this two-part documentary, the popular presenters uncover their surprising and emotional family histories, using a combination of DNA and genealogy. The shock revelations include military men in both of their families - one a medal-winning war hero, the other a deserter. And as well as delving into the past, Ant and Dec also get to meet DNA relatives they never knew existed

programme.title: The Savoy
programme.desc: The staff prepare to let their hair down at their annual gala, where they celebrate their most profitable year and busiest season. It's also where the prestigious Front of House star award will be announced - and concierge Dean, who has been nominated, practises his dance moves in between dealing with guests. Meanwhile, Savoy Grill director Thierry prepares for another visit from owner Gordon Ramsay and quality executive Debra does her daily rounds to check things are in perfect condition. But there is a crisis looming as news of Covid-19 reaches the hotel

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: When a conman and his girlfriend trick a prominent scientist with a tale about hidden treasure, he is taken hostage in Hamburg and sold to the Russians. Templar is 'lured' along similar lines and the confidence trick enables the spy to find the missing man. Roger Moore stars

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's holiday is disrupted by the escape of a violent psychopath from prison. Tim Cook, dubbed 'The Leopard' by the press has vowed murderous revenge on the detective inspector for his earlier capture, and has friends on the outside prepared to harbour him while he plans his attack. Crime drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: A pair of assassins perfect a chemical that kills all types of wood and plan to release it after killing members of the Institute of Timber. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: A face from the past spreads stories that cause Arthur and Dave to fall out, and the camel-coated entrepreneur opens a rival establishment to the Winchester Club. However, Ray harbours doubts concerning the instigator's honesty. George Cole, Gary Webster and Glynn Edwards star

programme.title: The Return of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Crusty senior diplomat Mycroft Holmes enlists the help of his more famous younger brother Sherlock to recover missing submarine plans vital to national security. Guest starring Charles Gray

programme.title: Live Snooker: Players Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the afternoon session on day five of the ranking tournament, featuring the concluding quarter-final, played over the best of 11 frames at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. Presented by Jill Douglas, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Ken Doherty, Neal Foulds and Rob Walker, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.title: The Avengers
programme.desc: Steed and King pose as a married couple hoping to enrol their son into the Alpha Academy for gifted youngsters. In reality however, the Academy appears to be a training facility with extra-terrestrial intentions. Patrick Macnee and Linda Thorson star

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Badgered by Ray over his methods of running the firm and seduced by a contemporary business rival about the virtues of retirement, Arthur decides to hand across Daley Enterprises to his nephew. However, in the event neither finds the move easy to handle. Liz Fraser guest stars, alongside George Cole and Gary Webster

programme.title: Live Snooker: Players Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the evening session on day five of the ranking tournament, featuring the opening semi-final, played over the best of 11 frames at Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes. Eventual champion Judd Trump was made to work hard for a 6-5 victory over Stephen Maguire at this stage last year in Southport. Presented by Jill Douglas, with analysis from Stephen Hendry, Alan McManus, Ken Doherty, Neal Foulds and Rob Walker, and commentary by David Hendon and Phil Yates

programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley.

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Promoted to Acting Lieutenant, Hornblower is soon forced to question his readiness for command. Meanwhile, the Indefatigable encounters another British ship that has been attacked by a Spanish frigate, leaving only three survivors _ one of whom is a notorious captain known to Pellew. Second of four 18th-century seafaring adventures based on the stories by CS Forester, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay, Denis Lawson, Ian McNeice and Andrew Tiernan

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: With just over two weeks until Cheltenham 2021, Oli Bell and the team look ahead to some jump racing at Kempton Park, plus some all-weather action from Lingfield Park

programme.desc: Action from November 1979, featuring Crystal Palace v Manchester City, Derby County v West Bromwich Albion and Sunderland v Chelsea

programme.title: ITV Football Classics
programme.desc: Elton Welsby presents extended highlights of the memorable match at Anfield, which saw Arsenal needing to win by at least two goals to pip their opponents to the title. With analysis from Bobby Robson, reports by Jim Rosenthal and commentary from Brian Moore and David Pleat

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A bloody attack on a young swimmer points to something dangerous in a remote stretch of the Parana River in Argentina. Jeremy Wade discovers locals are blaming a creature they call the River Tiger - a muscular predator with jaws like a bolt cutter - but to back up their stories, the freshwater detective needs to catch a specimen big enough to inflict the chilling wounds he has heard about

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Beyond the Amazon, in the remote jungles of Guyana, fishermen are disappearing without trace. Locals blame the 'water mama', a mermaid that is said to drag victims to their death. Could the largely unexplored rivers of this region be hiding a new monster, or is there a more familiar predator lurking beneath the surface? When an eyewitness claims to have seen the creature in a lake, Jeremy Wade's hunt for this mysterious bodysnatcher takes an unexpected turn, bringing him face to face with an astonishing beast

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of the 2.25 Close Brothers Adonis Juvenile Hurdle Race from Kempton Park, and races at 1.50, 3.00 and 3.35. Plus, races at 2.05 and 2.40 from Lingfield Park, and at 3.15 from Newcastle. With analysis from Alice Plunkett, Luke Harvey and Adele Mulrennan, reports by Matt Chapman, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

But what kind of creature could have inflicted such a distinctive, surgical wound? In his quest to expose the unseen mutilator, Jeremy uncovers multiple victims who've been mauled, half-blinded, and even fatally wounded by the same mysterious assailant - a baby-faced killer that appears to be proliferating

programme.title: River Monsters

Could the largely unexplored rivers of this region be hiding a new monster, or is there a more familiar predator lurking beneath the surface? When an eyewitness claims to have seen the creature in a lake, Jeremy Wade's hunt for this mysterious bodysnatcher takes an unexpected turn, bringing him face to face with an astonishing beast

programme.desc: Keen for revenge, Stephen and Alison set out on a road trip to track down Andrew's birth mother. Alison's newly single mum Wendy is struggling to settle in at the Barleycorn, Geoff faces a dilemma when he is offered a fortune to sell the picturesque burial plot next to his late wife's, and a rescue dog takes a surprising interest in Ellen. Black comedy, starring David Mitchell and Robert Webb

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Socially distanced trio Adam Hills, Josh Widdicombe and Alex Brooker are joined by guests Harry Hill and Judi Love for a comic review of the significant moments of the past seven days. As usual, the hashtag #isitok paves the way for the gang to round up, examine and explain the leading and most entertaining news stories of the week

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Omnibus. Felix, Warren and Brody await their arrest after the news about Cormac. Meanwhile, Cher gets into trouble with Sylver and takes her anger out on Romeo

programme.title: Jamie: Keep Cooking and Carry On

programme.desc: Sheldon tries to extend his lifespan, concerned he will not be around long enough for his consciousness to be downloaded into a robot body, and the friends encounter Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak (guest starring as himself) at the Cheesecake Factory

programme.desc: Sheldon tries to extend his lifespan, concerned he will not be around long enough for his consciousness to be downloaded into a robot body, and the friends encounter Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak (guest starring as himself) at the Cheesecake Factory

programme.desc: Lisa resolves to pursue a career in astronomy, but unfortunately her decision coincides with Chief Wiggum's hare-brained new initiative to reduce crime in Springfield - by illuminating the whole town all night. With the guest voice of Eric Idle

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer has his driving licence revoked for being a menace to other road-users, forcing Marge to take charge of the family car and run endless errands for everyone - a responsibility she soon starts to resent. Featuring the guest voices of Steve Busmen and singer-songwriter Jackson Browne

programme.title: A Taste of Italy
programme.desc: Chef and restaurateur Nisha Katona travels to the sunny port town of Bari in the Puglia region, where she is introduced to family recipes which are more than 400 years old. Visiting Nonna Nunzia's house, Nisha discovers orecchiette con cima di rapa - handmade orecchiette pasta with broccoli

programme.title: Live Test Cricket
programme.desc: India v England. Rishi Persad presents coverage of day five of the Third Test in the four-match series, held at Sardar Patel Stadium in Ahmedabad. India have lost just two of the 12 Tests they have played at this venue, but six of those contests ended as draws. With analysis from Alastair Cook

programme.title: Live Test Cricket
programme.desc: India v England. Rishi Persad presents coverage of day five of the Third Test in the four-match series, held at Sardar Patel Stadium in Ahmedabad. programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: After caring for her husband for the past seven years, Dorothy Pinkett searches for a property which would provide them with some respite on the Balearic Island of Menorca

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud follows the progress of musician Toby Leeming as he sets out to build a house wrapped around an ancient oak tree in rural Suffolk. The design for Toby's five-building home contains six bedrooms, six bathrooms, a music studio and an annexe - a vast project more like a village than a house. Creating this life changing vision on a £530k budget, and in only eight months, will not be an easy task, especially as they must build up close to an ancient oak without destroying any of the roots

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Road Trip
programme.desc: Fred Sirieix embarks on a road trip around some of Europe's most beautiful locations, meeting the Brits who have set up lives abroad. In the first edition, he sets out on a tour of southern Spain's whitewashed villages in Andalucía, where three sets of expats each run beautiful boutique hotels. He meets a couple with a grand plan to host weddings, and another learning on the job. Plus, can a single mum juggle work and family?

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: It's the quarter-final, and the remaining potters face a challenge inspired by Native American Acoma pottery that involves firing their pots in cow dung. Judges Rich Miller and Keith Brymer Jones set a tricky second challenge to throw an Alabama ring bottle in this all-American week, but who will secure an all-important place in the semi-final? Siobhan McSweeney hosts

programme.title: The Great

Comedy drama, starring Liam James, Sam Rockwell, Steve Carell, Toni Collette and Allison Janney

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George visits a Grade-II listed worker's cottage in Hampshire, as his mission to future-proof historic houses continues. Due to restrictions on planning permissions, the property has become somewhat disorganised, and is in need of a careful makeover. Seeking inspiration, George tours a Victorian stable that was renovated to add a gym, cinema and roof garden, and heads to a farmhouse in East Sussex to view a smart and stylish solution to the problem of designing new, accessible bathrooms for period properties

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Part one of two. The singer takes on Australia and New Zealand, beginning her adventures in Sydney, where she experiences the highlights on an open top bus tour. Next stop is a backstage pass to the world-famous Sydney Opera house. Next stop is Tasmania, where she visits a local wildlife sanctuary and its volunteers, comes face to face with its furry residents, and has a run-in with a cheeky wombat. Her first step onto New Zealand soil is at Dunedin, which is rich in Scottish heritage. Later, she heads on to Christchurch, a city devastated by earthquakes

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Neighbours

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: The Gadget Show
programme.desc: Georgie Barrat compares 1998's Nokia 5110 against Samsung's brand new S21 in a bid to assess how far mobile phone technology has moved on in 23 years. Jon Bentley is on double duty, first finding out which smart video doorbell is the best value, before going head to head with Craig Charles pitting corded and cordless vacuum cleaners against one another. Plus, Ortis Deley helps an interior designer who wants to see how tech can help in giving her home a much-needed makeover

programme.title: Kate Humble's Coastal Britain
programme.desc: On a 15-mile walk in North Yorkshire, Kate sets off from the picturesque settlement of Robin Hood's Bay, where archaeologist Maria explains how, in the 18th century, the fishing village's position made it an idea drop-off point for contraband shipped from Holland and France. From here, Kate heads south along the coast, meeting up with geologist Will, who reveals what dinosaur footprints can reveal about the creatures that once roamed the area. After arriving at her destination in Scarborough, Kate dons her wetsuit to join a group of swimmers braving the North Sea

programme.title: Gregg Wallace: Big Weekends Away
programme.desc: The MasterChef judge visits Scotland's capital, where he heads up the Royal Mile to one of the most documented castles in the world, goes underground to see the centuries-old streets that still remain and learns about the tradition of bagpipe playing. He also learns about Scotland's rich larder with one of its finest chefs Tom Kitchen, helping cook up a feast of grouse and langoustines. Gregg finishes his brief jaunt by joining some locals for an early-morning dip in the North Sea

programme.title: Britain's Favourite 80s Songs
programme.desc: Gary Davies narrates this look at the music of 1980, featuring revealing interviews with the singers and band members who made it one of the biggest years in pop. From the Specials and the Jam to Blondie and Bowie, and not forgetting the biggest girl band of the time - the Nolans, it was a year of music and pop videos to remember. Sheena Easton was singing about her man working 9 to 5, Madness stormed the charts with Baggy Trousers and Dexy's Midnight Runners had a memorable number one with Geno. Contributors include Toyah Willcox, Paul Gambaccini and Katie Puckrik

programme.title: That's So
programme.desc: An entertaining look back at events of 1980, a year politics met sport when the USA boycotted the Moscow Olympics. There was a taste of terrorist acts to come when the Iranian embassy in London was besieged prior to being stormed by the SAS in front of a live TV audience. Plus, Lady Diana Spencer emerged as a favourite of the paparazzi, B-movie actor Ronald Reagan beat Jimmy Carter in the US presidential election and prime minister Margaret Thatcher delivered her 'The lady's not for turning' speech. New developments in consumer technology saw the release of Pac-Man and the UK launch of the Sony Walkman

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

She tries to show him he still has something to live for, but he fears becoming a burden and considers ending his life. Romantic drama based on Jojo Moyes' novel, starring Emilia Clarke, Sam Claflin, Charles Dance and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: Me Before You (2016)
programme.desc: A woman hired as a carer for an embittered man who has been paralysed for the past two years following a motorcycle accident. Romantic drama based on Jojo Moyes' novel, starring Emilia Clarke, Sam Claflin, Charles Dance and Jenna Coleman

programme.title: 5 News Weekend
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Cruising with Jane McDonald
programme.desc: Traversing the Baltic on board the Viking Sky, Jane takes full advantage of the ship's amenities. Her first stop is Tallinn in Estonia, a medieval town with much ancient architecture. Next up is St Petersburg in Russia, where Jane heads to the Hermitage Museum, hoping to spot the cats that traditionally live in the basement. The third stop is Helsinki in Finland, where Jane meets a woman whose ambition is to kiss a brown bear. Finally, the ship pulls into the Swedish capital of Stockholm for one of the highlights of the singer's journey - a guided tour around the Abba Museum

programme.title: Inside the World's Greatest Hotels
programme.desc: The Ritz ranks high among London's most legendary hotels, one that has been synonymous with luxury and wealth, as well as being a bastion of privilege for over 100 years. Situated on Piccadilly, just a stone's throw from Buckingham Palace, it has been a home from home for the royal family since the reign of Edward VII. The Ritz has redefined service in luxury hotels, introducing the now-universal concept of the customer always being right. This programme examines its heritage, its famous afternoon teas and the scandalous history of its founder, Cesar Ritz

programme.title: Zara and Anne: Like Mother, Like Daughter
programme.desc: Documentary examining the relationship between Princess Anne and her daughter, using archive footage and interviews with historians, royal correspondents and insiders to reveal how Zara Tindall has been brought up to follow in her mother's footsteps. The programme looks at what makes them the royal family's unique mother and daughter duo, an if Zara ever escape her mother's firm grip

programme.title: Beatrice and Eugenie: Pampered Princesses?
programme.desc: A profile of the sisters who have been vilified, mocked, satirised and trolled for their clothes, looks, privilege and holidays, but probably most of all for simply being the children of the Duke and Duchess of York. The documentary looks at how close to reality public perception of the princesses actually is, with archive footage and interviews with royal insiders and people who know them

programme.title: Million Pound Homes
programme.desc: A look at the country's most impressive motorhomes, beginning with Charlotte and David's. They share a passion for travel and now are having their own overlander built, an off-road motorhome that can deal with any terrain. Plus, a look at The Icon, built by Formula One champion Jacques Villeneuve, which rents out for £4,000 a night

programme.title: Wonderful World of Chocolate
programme.desc: Documentary offering a glimpse inside the confectionery world, visiting factories, chocolatiers and shops, and even revealing the Queen's favourite chocolates

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Julian Norton is called out to a desperately ill calf, and Peter Wright treats an elderly cat with severe constipation. Other patients include a young wild stag trapped by its antlers, a dog with a dislocated leg, a postnatal cow and five noisy young goats whose horn buds need removing

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Mosque
programme.desc: Meera Syal narrates a documentary going behind the scenes at the Baitul Futuh mosque in Morden, south London, home to Britain's 30,000-strong Ahmadi Muslim community. The film reveals the defining moments of life at the mosque, from finding love to committing to faith as well as looking at international celebrations and a tragedy that devastates the entire community

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: Documentary about the work of dedicated animal lovers who save injured and orphaned wild animals brought into their sanctuary

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Dr Paul Binski investigates places of worship designed by Christopher Wren after the Great Fire of London in 1666. Assigned to rebuild 51 of the city's churches, he created a trademark open, auditorium-style feel to emphasise the shift from Catholic to Anglican dominance

programme.title: House Doctor

programme.title: The Secret Life of Puppies

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Peppa Pig

programme.title: Brave Bunnies

programme.title: Little Princess

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa

programme.desc: Mr Krabs makes SpongeBob and Squidward clean up Bikini Bottom. A sea bunny ravages Squidward's garden

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel gives advice on being more independent to her pampered younger sister (Reese Witherspoon), who promptly makes her regret it by flirting with Ross. Meanwhile, Joey gets in trouble for giving free food to female customers at Central Perk

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Phoebe accidentally becomes a porn icon, Rachel tries to stop her sister from embarking on a relationship with Ross, and Chandler reveals he is unable to cry. Guest starring Reese Witherspoon

programme.title: Friends

A scientist learns the world is soon to face a global cataclysm that will wipe out the human race, but finds the US president has plans to secretly save a fraction of the population, leaving everyone else to die. A writer learns of this scheme, and as the world is devastated by earthquakes and volcanic eruptions, he tries to get his family to the last safe refuge. Disaster movie, with John Cusack and Chiwetel Ejiofor

programme.title: 5 News Update
programme.desc: Round-up of the latest headlines, sports news and weather

A scientist learns the world is soon to face a global cataclysm that will wipe out the human race, but finds the US president has plans to secretly save a fraction of the population, leaving everyone else to die. A writer learns of this scheme, and as the world is devastated by earthquakes and volcanic eruptions, he tries to get his family to the last safe refuge. Disaster movie, with John Cusack and Chiwetel Ejiofor

programme.title: Million Pound Homes
programme.desc: Documentary following people who invest millions in the ultimate floating home or holiday bolthole, including a barge worth £2.5million in one of the UK's most expensive locations. Ex-City trader Rob isn't afraid of a challenge. After a tough job almost caused him to have a breakdown, he stepped away to figure out a new life. He gambled big and invested in an old barge - he spent £500,000 on renovations and hopes to sell it for a cool million

programme.title: Rich House, Poor House
programme.desc: Businessman Adam and his father Ian, who live in a four-bedroom mansion in Essex, swap with single mum Kiptieu, who lives in a small council flat in Abbey Wood, London. With a number of successful businesses under his belt, Adam now travels round the world, interviewing the great and the good from showbusiness, sport and business, including the likes of John Travolta, 50 Cent and Floyd Mayweather, sharing their stories of success with clients who are willing to pay Adam thousands for the inside track. Kiptieu fled war-torn Sierra Leone and is a widow bringing up her three daughters in a two-bedroom ground-floor flat. Kiptieu also recently lost her job because of the Covid-19 pandemic, so the family budget is tighter than ever

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Documentary following British people living it up on the Costa del Sol. Bambi shows newcomers Howard and Lynne around the caravan park and treats them to a Bollywood evening. But unaware to the couple the host has recently gone vegan, so it turns out to be a meat-free knees-up. Kim and Matt reopen their chip shop after a devastating fire which forced them to shut for a year and Harrison is down on the marina, trying to sell tickets for the Black Pearl pirate ship tour

programme.title: About Time (2013)
programme.desc: A single man gets the chance to turn his unhappy life around when his father reveals that they both have the ability to travel through time and change their personal history. He uses his newfound talent to try to improve his love life and win the heart of an American woman he has fallen for. Romantic fantasy comedy, with Domhnall Gleeson, Rachel McAdams and Bill Nighy

programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: Documentary about Yorkshire vet Julian Norton, his partner Peter Wright and their team. Julian gets dragged around a barn by one-ton bull Merchant and operates on police dog Thor. Peter helps a baby goat open its eyes and fixes a cat with loose teeth

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: A farmer brings in one of his much-loved semi-feral cats for treatment, with tricky surgery required to remove a lump on its face that could be a life-threatening tumour. Julian helps ewe Vera to give birth, while Peter and Sarah encounter Alfie, a cocky young donkey who has unusual parenting issues

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Paul Binski chronicles the history of Britain's religious art, examining the legacies of two wealthy industrialists of the 17th and 18th centuries who demonstrated a flamboyant taste for kitsch in the baroque forms of churches in Great Witley, Worcestershire, and London's Little Stanmore

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Californian interior designer and estate agent Ann Maurice visits a five-bedroom house on the market in Northwich, Cheshire, and advises the owners on making it more attractive to would-be buyers

programme.title: 7th Cavalry (1956)
programme.desc: An army officer is branded a coward for failing to fight in the Battle of the Little Bighorn, and is unable to convince anyone that official business had detained him. In a bid to repair his damaged reputation, he leads a cavalry detail to retrieve the dead from the battlefield. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Jay C Flippen and Jeanette Nolan

programme.title: Buchanan Rides Alone (1958)
programme.desc: A drifter rides into a town dominated by the members of a ruthless family. He befriends a Mexican in jail for killing a man who attacked his sister and tries to save him from the gallows, getting caught up in the evil clan's running internal conflicts in the process. Western, starring Randolph Scott, Craig Stevens, Manuel Rojas and Barry Kelley

programme.title: The Man Who Would Be King (1975)
programme.desc: Two British soldiers in 19th-century India travel to an unexplored land, where one of them fools the natives into believing he is a god. However, his growing delusions of grandeur put him at loggerheads with his comrade-in-arms. Adventure based on the Rudyard Kipling story, directed by John Huston, starring Sean Connery, Michael Caine, Saeed Jaffrey, Christopher Plummer and Shakira Caine

programme.title: The Return of the Pink Panther (1975)
programme.desc: The inept Inspector Clouseau sets out to unmask a notorious international jewel thief believed to be behind the theft of a priceless diamond - but anarchy, as ever, follows wherever he goes. Blake Edwards' comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Christopher Plummer, Catherine Schell, Herbert Lom, Burt Kwouk and Graham Stark

programme.title: Nutty Professor II: The Klumps (2000)
programme.desc: Bumbling scientist Sherman Klump is engaged to be married and determined to rid himself of his troublesome alter ego Buddy Love for ever. All his problems look set to be solved by his fiancee's latest DNA experiment - but things are about to spiral out of control. Comedy sequel, starring Eddie Murphy in multiple roles, along with Janet Jackson and Larry Miller

programme.title: Upgrade (2018)
programme.desc: Technophobe Grey Trace is left confined to a wheelchair after being mugged. His only hope for revenge is Stem, an experimental computer chip implant that allows him to control his body. However, once implanted, Stem starts talking to Trace and takes over his body. Sci-fi thriller from Saw veteran Leigh Whannell, starring Logan Marshall-Green and Melanie Vallejo

programme.title: Punisher: War Zone (2008)
programme.desc: The ruthless vigilante considers hanging up his guns when he learns a gangster he killed was actually an undercover FBI agent. However, he must return to his war on crime when a mobster who was hideously disfigured at his hands assembles a gang of violent criminals to cause mayhem on the streets of New York. Action thriller based on the Marvel comic-book, starring Ray Stevenson and Dominic West

programme.title: Double Jeopardy (1999)
programme.desc: A man fakes his own death and frames his wife for murder, but after six years in prison she is released. Since the woman has already served her time, she has nothing to lose by really killing him - but her mission of vengeance is complicated by a zealous parole officer determined to stop her. Thriller, starring Ashley Judd, Tommy Lee Jones, Bruce Greenwood and Annabeth Gish

programme.title: Horton Hears a Who! (2008)
programme.desc: A kindly elephant hears a cry of help coming from a speck of dust and discovers the minuscule city of Whoville. As he tries to help its citizens, the other animals begin to question his sanity, and the evil Kangaroo incites them to turn against the do-gooder. Animated adaptation of Dr Seuss's book, with the voices of Jim Carrey, Steve Carell, Carol Burnett, Seth Rogen and Will Arnett

programme.title: Capture the Flag (2015)
programme.desc: A young surfer and his friends try to prevent a devious plan to change the course of history when a ruthless billionaire sets out to stop the moon landings from happening, so that he can gain control of a valuable energy source. Animated sci-fi adventure, with the voices of Sam Fink, Phillipa Alexander and Lorraine Pilkington

programme.title: Carry On Regardless (1961)
programme.desc: A businessman starts up an odd-job agency, but employs seven hopeless handymen incapable of completing any task without causing utter chaos. Matters come to a head when their landlord tries to evict them - forcing the bungling workmen to finally prove their worth. Slapstick comedy, starring Sid James, Charles Hawtrey and Kenneth Williams

programme.title: Home Alone 3 (1997)
programme.desc: Cheeky eight-year-old Alex Pruitt is delighted to be given a remote-controlled toy car by his neighbour - unaware it contains a stolen microchip. A gang of crooks heads to Alex's town with the intention of recovering the valuable item, but the resourceful youngster has prepared for their arrival with a series of nasty booby-traps. Comedy sequel, starring Alex D Linz, Olek Krupa, Rya Kihlstedt and Lenny von Dohlen

programme.title: Ghostbusters (2016)
programme.desc: When a series of supernatural disturbances occurs in New York City, two experts in the paranormal investigate, joined by a brilliant inventor who has created an array of ghost-hunting gadgets and a public transport worker whose knowledge of the city provides vital clues to the sinister cause of the ghostly activity. Reboot of the 1980s comedy franchise, starring Kristen Wiig, Melissa McCarthy, Kate McKinnon, Leslie Jones and Chris Hemsworth

programme.title: Red Sparrow (2018)
programme.desc: Ballerina Dominika Egorova is recruited to a Russian intelligence service, where she is forced to use her body as a weapon. Her first mission, targeting a CIA agent, threatens to unravel the security of both nations. Spy thriller, starring Jennifer Lawrence, Joel Edgerton and Matthias Schoenaerts

programme.title: Carrie (1976)
programme.desc: A shy, withdrawn teenager discovers she has paranormal powers, which she uses to protect herself from her abusive mother and classmates. When a friendly student invites her to the prom, she is determined to enjoy herself and is amazed when she is crowned queen of the ball - but joy turns to fury when her tormentors play a cruel prank. Brian De Palma's adaptation of Stephen King's horror novel, with Sissy Spacek, Piper Laurie and Amy Irving

programme.title: Shallow Grave (1994)
programme.desc: Three flat-sharing friends rent out their spare room to an enigmatic stranger, who promptly dies of an overdose, leaving behind a suitcase crammed full of cash. They plot to dispose of the body and keep the money - but as a curious detective comes looking for the dead man, the flatmates turn on one another. Danny Boyle's thriller, starring Ewan McGregor, Christopher Eccleston and Kerry Fox

programme.title: Edward Scissorhands (1990)
programme.desc: A reclusive genius creates a boy but dies before completing him, leaving his brainchild with scissor-like blades in place of hands. A kindly woman from a nearby suburb takes pity on the lonely figure and welcomes him into her home. He quickly becomes a hit in the neighbourhood - but then his new friends start to turn against him. Tim Burton's fantasy, starring Johnny Depp, Winona Ryder, Dianne Wiest and Vincent Price

programme.title: The Book of Life (2014)
programme.desc: A man who longs to be a musician instead of a bullfighter like his father has to compete with a childhood friend when they fall for the same woman. Their love triangle catches the eye of the spirits of the dead, leading him on a journey through their bizarre world. Animated adventure, with the voices of Diego Luna, Zoe Saldana and Channing Tatum

programme.title: Bee Movie (2007)
programme.desc: A bee wearies of a life dedicated to producing honey and decides to leave his hive. During his foray into the wider world, he befriends a florist. However, when he realises what becomes of the honey his species toils so hard to make, he resolves to sue the entire human race for stealing from his fellow creatures. Animated comedy, with the voices of Jerry Seinfeld, Renee Zellweger and Chris Rock

programme.title: The Pink Panther Strikes Again (1976)
programme.desc: Inspector Clouseau fends off a murder syndicate led by his former police chief Dreyfus, who has been driven insane by his hatred for the bungling detective. However, doing away with the lucky lawman proves harder than anticipated - even for the world's best assassins. Comedy sequel, starring Peter Sellers, Herbert Lom, Lesley-Anne Down, Burt Kwouk and Leonard Rossiter

programme.title: Behind Enemy Lines (2001)
programme.desc: A reconnaissance pilot is shot down and becomes stranded in enemy territory after filming evidence of a genocidal massacre by Serb militia in Bosnia. The naval air commander for Nato forces in the Balkans defies orders by mounting an unauthorised mission to rescue him, while the airman faces a battle to stay alive as he is pursued by paramilitary forces. Bosnian War drama, starring Gene Hackman, Owen Wilson, Gabriel Macht and Olek Krupa

programme.title: Gifted (2017)
programme.desc: Single man Frank Adler attempts to raise his child prodigy niece, Mary. However, his plans for a normal school life for the youngster are foiled when the youngster's mathematical abilities come to the attention of Frank's formidable mother, Evelyn. Drama, starring Chris Evans, Lindsay Duncan and Mckenna Grace

programme.title: Bad Neighbours 2 (2016)
programme.desc: A couple who have finally rid themselves of the college fraternity next door become stuck with even worse new neighbours in the shape of a raucous college sorority. In desperation, they turn to their old enemies in getting rid of the new arrivals. Comedy, starring Seth Rogen, Zac Efron, Rose Byrne and Chloe Grace Moretz

programme.title: Secrets & Lies (1996)
programme.desc: A successful black optician learns she is adopted and goes in search of her birth mother, who turns out to be a working class white woman, estranged from her brother and struggling to connect with her teenage daughter. The reunited mother and child soon become close friends, but tensions rise at a family gathering. Mike Leigh's drama, starring Brenda Blethyn, Marianne Jean-Baptiste and Timothy Spall

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Beaver, Pennsylvania, where he meets a brother-and-sister team who find it almost impossible to work together. Their relationship is so fractured that they're failing to save their father's Italian restaurant Levanti's from being overwhelmed by local competition, and the situation has become so desperate that their dad is in danger of losing his life savings

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Rainbow has been keeping in touch with Dre's godbrother Omar while he is in prison, but when the family finds out that Omar is being released, his future sparks some debate. Ruby lets it slip to Andre Jr that she has been to jail, so he pushes her to reveal more secrets from her past

programme.title: Black-ish
programme.desc: Dre is chosen to lead Stevens & Lido's new charity campaign helping people give back to their community, but when Rainbow points out there's more to giving back than writing cheques, he donates some of his clothes to a man in need. Andre Jr fails his driving test, so Ruby offers to act as chaperone when he takes the twins out on a trip

programme.title: Malcolm in the Middle
programme.desc: Hal hosts a poker night and the boys take it upon themselves to entertain a player's daughter, but Malcolm discovers one guest is carrying a gun in her bag. Family comedy, starring Frankie Muniz and Bryan Cranston

programme.title: Malcolm in the Middle
programme.desc: The family car is damaged, but the boys refuse to admit responsibility - making Hal so exasperated that he forces them to see a psychiatrist. Comedy, starring Bryan Cranston and Frankie Muniz

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard and Howard attempt to distract Sheldon with a trip to a historic railway when he slows the progress of their work on the Air Force guidance system. Raj and Stuart look after baby Halley, while Penny and Amy take Bernadette out

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is furious when she learns that Sheldon has shared intimate details of their relationship with his colleagues at the university, prompting their first fight since moving in together. Raj considers trying to live without financial support from his father, who thinks he is spoilt

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: When Mr Meller singles out Adam during dodgeball, Beverly intervenes and gets him fired. Soon after, Murray and Adam see Meller in a new light, so Beverly tries to get his job back for him. Meanwhile, Barry can't accept that Erica is better than him at a new board game - Trivial Pursuit

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly challenges Erica to participate in Jazzercise after she claims her mother's obsession is 'sad'. However, when she wins the bet, Erica opts out of celebrating Thanksgiving so Beverly has to find a way to win her daughter back to the celebrations. Uncle Marvin visits and unexpectedly bonds with Adam. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey and Jeff Garlin

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Gina spends time with each member of the squad, playing a high-stakes game of chess with Captain Holt, going out for a fancy lunch with Diaz and Santiago, infiltrating an expensive social club with Peralta and finding special gifts for the team

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jeffords believes the Pontiac Bandit is up to his old tricks and stealing cars again, but Peralta is convinced he's wrong and attempts to prove it. The rest of the squad battles to keep Shaw's bar a police hangout after some firefighters stake their claim to it

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard is invited to visit the Large Hadron Collider in Switzerland and asks Penny to join him as a Valentine's Day treat. However, Sheldon tries to persuade the couple to allow him to take her place

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon misses an opportunity to meet Spider-Man co-creator Stan Lee when he has to go to court for driving through a red light, and his anger leads to his imprisonment. Penny finds out the address of the comic-book writer and takes Sheldon there - but his troubles with the law have yet to reach their conclusion and the visit does not go as expected

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and Amy's attempts to celebrate her birthday with an intimate night at home are interrupted by the news that Bernadette has gone into labour. However, it transpires that Howard, Raj and Stuart may have rushed the expectant mother to the hospital too early, with Raj having difficulty keeping the gender of the unborn child a secret

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Murray takes Adam to a Phillies baseball game and allows him to leave his seat on his own for the first time, only to find Adam immediately gets lost in the stadium. Beverly accompanies Erica and Barry on a shopping trip - but they begin arguing on the way home

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Erica decides to put college on hold and pursue her dream of becoming a pop singer - which causes her parents no end of stress. Meanwhile, an April Fools' Day prank leads to an all-out war between Adam and Barry. Comedy, starring Wendi McLendon-Covey, Jeff Garlin and Hayley Orrantia

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta and the squad must track down a hacker who has infiltrated the precinct's servers, compromising protected information on undercover agents. Rosa deals with relationship issues, while Hitchcock and Scully attempt to cook the perfect lasagne

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Jeffords becomes anxious about his upcoming lieutenant's exam, Peralta, Captain Holt and the squad decide to distract him from the stress by holding the precinct's annual Halloween Heist on a new date, Cinco de Mayo

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The last couple say their final vows - but with their relationship in turmoil, will they go through with it?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is forced to teach a course at the university, and Howard surprises the gang when he decides to enrol on it. Meanwhile, Amy plays Penny and Bernadette off against each other while the tension brews over Penny's new job. Comedy, guest starring Regina King (Southland), with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon tries to extend his lifespan, concerned he will not be around long enough for his consciousness to be downloaded into a robot body, and the friends encounter Apple co-founder Steve Wozniak (guest starring as himself) at the Cheesecake Factory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon breaks up with Amy and turns to cats in his search for an alternative to human companionship. However, when he starts to bulk-buy pets, Leonard decides enough is enough and calls for reinforcements to help bring his friend back to his senses. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Drama following the lives and loves of a group of teenagers and young adults in Chester

programme.title: Below Deck Mediterranean
programme.desc: After Ben apologises for shutting down Bryan and Tiffany's fun, the crew prepares for its next charter hailing from Oklahoma. The stews are tasked with putting together a country hoedown complete with bales of hay, and the deck crew unravels when Danny accuses Bobby for not being part of the team. After hours, the charter guests seem to have a threesome in the jacuzzi and once again, Danny gets himself into hot water for overstepping his boundaries

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: Six weeks after the end of the realty experiment, all 24 participants reunite for the very last dinner party

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Twenty six-year-old psychology student Alesia is tired of meeting 'boys' and is now seeking someone more emotionally mature than her previous partners. Next up is Louise from London, who has travelled the world and been on over 200 dates, but is still no closer to finding her soul mate. Anna Richardson assists both singletons, inviting them to choose their favourite partner from a line-up of potential dates who reveal themselves one body part at a time until fully nude. The contenders then don their glad rags to enjoy a date together

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: The fly-on-the-wall series turning the cameras on TV's most vocal armchair critics features their thoughts on programmes from September 2018. Shows under consideration include talent show The X Factor, espionage drama Bodyguard, documentary Reported Missing, science programme The Joy of AI and slimming series The Extreme Diet Hotel

programme.title: Celebs Go Dating
programme.desc: Amy Childs is all over her next date Jamie, while Dean Gaffney heads off to a prison-themed date, which ends with a dramatic escape. Lockie catches up with Pete Wicks to get some solid advice from the 'salty pirate'

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Doc Brown, Joe Wilkinson, Jon Richardson, Katherine Ryan and Richard Osman are confronted with an intimidating man in gold chains - the Mayor of Chesham. Greg Davies hosts

programme.title: Angel
programme.desc: Spike meets up with fellow Sunnydale survivor Andrew Wells when he and Angel travel to Italy to help out Buffy. Meanwhile, Fred's parents arrive at Wolfram & Hart looking for their daughter. Supernatural drama, starring David Boreanaz

programme.title: Schitt's Creek

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's friends all fall for his sexy twin sister, who is paying him a visit, but he declares he will have the final say on who can date her - an announcement that particularly dismays Leonard. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

Comedy, guest starring Regina King (Southland), with Johnny Galecki, Jim Parsons and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: When self-proclaimed ladies' man Jayden, 20, meets childminder Isabel, 19, things go well between them - until they realise they have a mutual friend. Sarah, 44, has been deaf since birth, and wants to find love with a hearing man, so she's relieved to find 47-year-old estate agent John is easy to lip read. Eternal romantic Tim returns to the restaurant, and his date, Sarah, shares his passion for bad jokes, exploding high fives and limericks. Disco-loving personal trainer Ellis, 47, is pleased when 53-year-old hairdresser Gary turns up, and his touching life story makes her warm to him even more. programme.title: Food Unwrapped

Kate Quilton finds out whether the makers of Marmite can really predict whether people will love or hate their distinctive yeast-based spread, as claimed in their latest TV advert, and Tebbutt aims to solve the age-old mystery of how to smoothly extract ketchup from a bottle - by running across a pond of custard

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: After 30 years of marriage and a life dedicated to their family, Simon and Mary Loyne have decided it's time to focus on themselves. They've sold their Cheshire home and cannot wait to move to Spain's Costa de Almería. Danni Menzies shows them five properties for their budget of £100,000, but do they go for a townhouse with a 'man cave' that Simon really wants, or an apartment close to the beach in Mary's perfect location?

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items including baby scales, an oil can and a glass cabinet that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Angel Adoree insists that Dick Strawbridge smash a hole through one of the castle's three-foot-thick interior walls to create a honeymoon suite, but he has far too much to do on his own and the schedule slips, badly

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton helps Barbara and her boyfriend David build a dream home in Peebles, Scotland, where the whole ground floor is devoted to stables for her horses. To keep costs down, the couple are living in a caravan on site, but their resolve is tested by winter temperatures of minus 10. The budget is tight and Charlie tries to help Barbara to make savings. However, when she runs out of cash, the future of the build hangs in the balance

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to The Waverley Hotel in Callander in Scotland, owned by husband and wife Gordon and Jan Halladay. Ollie and Liz discover that the bathroom bin hasn't been emptied and best friends Tanya and Vicky get the gloves out for a thorough examination. Later, Jan challenges the group to become shipmates for the day and Tanya takes a shine to Liz. Over dinner, the group realise that Jan is the boss in her marriage. The next day the guests are looking forward to finally meeting Gordon, who's cooking breakfast. However, his confidence slips away when he serves over-cooked poached eggs, and Jan and Gordon are shocked and surprised by some of their feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Greenview Guesthouse in Cumbria, owned by Tanya Briggs, with help from best friend Vicky Hope

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week takes place at the Hotel Sheraton in Blackpool, where the group are shocked by the sheer size of the hotel, run by mother and son Liz and Ollie Brown

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Katharine and Rory offer some proper Yorkshire hospitality at Chapter Two Accommodation in Malton, including an afternoon of sausage-making and a belly-busting breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Tanya defends the lack of breakfast offering at Greenview Guesthouse and is left flabbergasted when the payments are revealed

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The presenter continues his look back at some of the best builds over five series of Amazing Spaces. This time he turns his attention to some of the most innovative and eccentric caravan and campervan builds he's ever seen - from a tiny Italian tuk tuk campervan to a trailer tent caravan clad in old CDs, and a tiny 1950s ambulance converted to sleep a family of six, plus their dog. There is also an Airstream caravan converted into the ultimate party pad, and a look at George's own high-tech holiday home in the Lake District

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Pregnant rhino Asha goes into labour overnight, meaning there is a surprise waiting for the keepers when they arrive to work the next morning. Meanwhile, Malayan tapirs Margery and Betong say goodbye to their daughter, Solo, who is leaving to start a new life at a zoo in Germany, and female dead leaf mantis Minnie is looking for her first partner

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree make plans to host weddings in the grounds of their propery, while looking ahead to keeping livestock and growing their own crops. Dick takes steps to generate solar power during a hot summer, and turns an old oil tank into a pig roaster

programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: It's Garden Week in the pottery and the remaining potters face a green-fingered challenge - to build an animal water feature and throw a strawberry planter. The pressure's on to turn on the waterworks, as judges Keith Brymer Jones and Rich Miller decide who will make the quarter-finals and who will be heading home. Siobhan McSweeney hosts

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: Documentary examining the killing of British tourist Peter Falconio in Australia's Northern Territory in July 2001, exploring the details and questions that surround this infamous case. In this first episode, Andrew Fraser, formerly one of Australia's foremost criminal defence lawyers, begins the reinvestigation into the Peter Falconio story, visits the Outback crime scene and speaks to a leading forensic scientist and witnesses from the time of the murder, who raise serious questions about evidence in the case

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: A look at how sole eyewitness Joanne Lees came under immense scrutiny and pressure from both the British and Australian press as they hounded her continuously for an interview, while certain detectives were also sceptical of her version of events. The programme includes detailed sections of a video showing Joanne being confronted by detectives about seeming inconsistences in her account of the attack

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Four-year-old Alfie rushed in with a broken thighbone after falling off his bike, the medical team suspects 19-year-old rugby player Chris has suffered neck and spinal injuries, while James, 42, arrives with chest pains and breathing difficulties. Documentary following life inside one of Britain's busiest A&E departments at King's College Hospital in south London.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: The documentary follows the work of the stroke team at south London's King's College Hospital. Laura's family faces a big decision when the 72-year-old grandmother is found to have a blood clot on her brain, while father-of-three Nic, the over-40s National BMX Champion, collapses in reception after suffering an accident while training for a stunt. The medics also treat 83-year-old Marjorie, who is complaining of a pain behind her eye following a recent bout of shingles

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Jack, Dougal and Ted head off for a summer break at a caravan park. However, they immediately get on the wrong side of their fellow holiday-makers, and matters become even more dire when Father Noel Furlong turns up - with his hapless youth group in tow. Comedy, guest starring Graham Norton

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priests organise a raffle to raise funds to repair their leaky roof, planning to award a car as the prize. Unfortunately, Ted accidentally wrecks it before the grand draw takes place, and embarks on a frantic search for a replacement. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon play the Craggy Island clerics

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Four-year-old Alfie rushed in with a broken thighbone after falling off his bike, the medical team suspects 19-year-old rugby player Chris has suffered neck and spinal injuries, while James, 42, arrives with chest pains and breathing difficulties. Documentary following life inside one of Britain's busiest A&E departments at King's College Hospital in south London.

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Helen Lawal searches for the secret to perfectly shelled walnuts in California, and Matt Tebbutt hopes to discover what makes sweets sour. Andi Oliver investigates how the cloud gets into the hazy beers that are so trendy currently

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Jo and Ellis from East Sussex cannot wait to buy a holiday home on Spain's Costa Blanca, where they plan to spend every moment in the sunshine indulging their passion for water sports and mountain biking. Laura Hamilton leaves no stone unturned, showing them five properties for their budget of £60,000

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin shows Southampton couple David and Sonia five fantastic properties for their budget of £100,000 in the Almeria province of southern Spain

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Kent retirees David and Donna Bradley want to relocate permanently to Menorca, so Laura Hamilton shows them five properties for their £300,000 budget

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Trainee accountant Annika hosts the first dinner party in Boston, Lincolnshire, where she hopes to impress farmer's wife Carolyn, electrical engineer Adrian, carer Sylwia, and glamorous mum Katy with an evening modelled on a country fayre

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Competitions fanatic Katy hosts the second night in Boston, Lincolnshire, where she aims to impress her guests with her organisation skills and a menu featuring savoury profiteroles. However, fellow contestant Carolyn is critical of the food, and lone male host Adrian struggles to feel included when the conversation turns to hair extensions and fake eyelashes

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the third night of the competition in Boston, Lincolnshire, Adrian hopes that his spicy food and surprises will help him scoop the £1,000 prize. Although he was quiet at the table the previous night, Adrian goes on the charm offensive and seems to be impressing his guests - but he may have gone a step too far with his final revelation

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Polish-born carer Sylwia hosts the fourth party of the competition in Boston, Lincolnshire, and hopes her European menu will charm her guests. However, the growing friction between diners Annika and Carolyn threatens to spoil the evening, and the host's chances of winning the £1,000 prize

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the final dinner party night in Boston, and the hard-to-please foxhunter Carolyn is planning on using a charm offensive to win over the guests after having criticised each of them during the week. She has offended Annika, called Katy fake, found fault with Sylwia's food, and reprimanded Adie, but still believes she may be able to turn things around

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition begins at Alston House in Cumbria, where owners Michael and Carole Allchorne are brimming with confidence, but Yvonne Copeland and Gail Wells are less than impressed with the decor. The guests are impressed with chef Michael's breakfast, but the hosts are left reeling by the criticisms levelled at their hotel during the feedback session

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to Scotia House in Harrogate, where host Marie Lock is hoping her obsessive cleaning regime will give her the edge in the competition. In the rooms, both Michael and Carole and Yvonne and Gail find the deluxe teddy bears freaky, and for Billy and Billy, things are a little on the cosy side. In the afternoon, Marie's keen to show off her moves and pits her guests against each other in a Northern Soul dance off

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Canberra Guest House in Blackpool for the third day of the competition, where host Yvonne Copeland, joined by her part-time helper Gail Wells, hopes her cheap and cheerful B&B will win over her guests. However, Marie finds what she thinks is urine and hair on the toilet seat in her room, and a lack of wi-fi means Billy Junior has to put his business on hold. Yvonne takes her competitors to a local reptile house, but serious divisions unfold within the group, and later, tensions rise again at dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Crown and Kitchen in East Linton, East Lothian, owned by father and son Billy and Billy Jr Robertson, who see themselves as the jokers of the pack. Yvonne and Gail leave no mattress unturned in their room inspection, although Michael and Carole are impressed. Later, Yvonne makes quite a splash when the hosts organise an assault course for their guests, but slow service during breakfast leaves the B&Bers disappointed - especially when Billy Jr is at a loss to explain what the problem was

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. Things are tense from the start as Carole and Michael from Alston House challenge Marie on her comments. Next, Marie from Scotia House wants to set the record straight with allegations of tit-for-tat scoring. The group members proceed to thrash out their differences before the week's winners are revealed

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Documentary following Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree through the process of buying and restoring a French chateau that has been empty for the past four decades. Their new home has 12 acres of land, nine outbuildings, a walled garden and a moat - but it also has no electricity, heating or water, and proves to be a far more complicated DIY project than expected

programme.title: Darcey Bussell's Wild Coasts of Scotland
programme.desc: Darcey travels to Islay, from where she embarks on a boat trip to see Corryvreckan, the third largest whirlpool on Earth, and also visits Ardbeg Distillery to sample its whisky

programme.title: The Great British Dig: History in Your Back Garden
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis is joined by a team of expert archaeologists to excavate back gardens around Britain, uncovering the lost history buried beneath our lawns and flower beds. This time, Hugh and the team are in the market town of Masham in North Yorkshire, hoping to find a Viking burial ground hidden underneath

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Brian, 74, is brought into King's by ambulance after falling backwards down a flight of stairs and being knocked unconscious at his partner's house. Sylvester arrives in paediatric A&E with his two-year-old son, who has cut his forehead on a visit to church, while 70-year-old builder Leonard has lost sensation down his left side and the medics are worried he may have suffered a stroke

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Ellen, 86, has been brought in by ambulance after her daughters found her collapsed at home. Her body temperature has dropped dangerously low and she's also lost five pints of blood. Meanwhile, appendicitis is suspected when 75-year-old Martin is rushed in with severe abdominal pain, and neurologists perform tests to determine whether 70-year-old mother-of-10 Lula has suffered a stroke

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: In the third episode, former criminal defence lawyer Andrew Fraser questions the verdict of the murder trial, that saw Bradley Murdoch found guilty of murder. The programme examines other potential suspects, including an eyewitness account of a couple talking to a man who matched the police description of the killer, as well as considering theories that Falconio is still alive, since his body was never found and several people have reported encountering him

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr, Sean Lock and Jon Richardson are joined by Bob Mortimer and Holly Walsh for more comic innuendos and unpredictable conundrums. Claudia Winkleman stands in for Rachel Riley on letters-and-numbers duty, while Isy Suttie is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent is in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Craggy Island's most famous religious relic is due to be upgraded and Ted sets about trying to ensure the ceremony runs smoothly. However, Dougal and Jack put his plans in jeopardy when they meet the three priests in charge of proceedings. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Dougal allows himself to be led astray by rebellious new arrival Father Damien, and Ted is suspected of being the criminal mastermind behind a scandalous theft. Comedy, with Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Joe Rooney

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: Jack, Dougal and Ted head off for a summer break at a caravan park. However, they immediately get on the wrong side of their fellow holiday-makers, and matters become even more dire when Father Noel Furlong turns up - with his hapless youth group in tow. Comedy, guest starring Graham Norton

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Brian, 74, is brought into King's by ambulance after falling backwards down a flight of stairs and being knocked unconscious at his partner's house. Sylvester arrives in paediatric A&E with his two-year-old son, who has cut his forehead on a visit to church, while 70-year-old builder Leonard has lost sensation down his left side and the medics are worried he may have suffered a stroke

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect attends a carol service in a 130-year-old flat-pack tin church, while Will Hardie visits a very different chapel that has been transformed into a luxury home. The duo help an ice carver create a winter wonderland-themed bar in Liverpool, and the programme catches up with some of George's favourite builds of the year. There are also a few Christmas drinks in the garden of Joel Bird, winner of the Shed of the Year prize

programme.title: Ugly House to Lovely House with George Clarke
programme.desc: Architect Chris Dyson helps Wendy and Allan in Colchester, who are desperate to transform their horrible-looking 1960s home into something more modern. Adamant that the house needs a drastic makeover externally and a major reconfiguration internally, can Chris satisfy their high expectations while sticking to their £150,000 budget?

programme.title: George Clarke's Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George Clarke gets to work on a 300-year-old house in Broadstairs, which was once a school and a retirement community, leaving it with a few quirky features - not least a row of three toilets. For inspiration, he visits the Royal Pavilion in Brighton. He also helps a couple make the most of the tiny living room in their 1930s south London bungalow

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Surrey, five cooks from the Woking and Weybridge area battle to win the culinary contest's £1,000 prize. The first to host is Vincenzo Ferlita, who works in an ice-cream van. Staying true to his Italian and Sicilian roots, he hopes that his wealth of family recipes will impress his guests

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Canadian housewife and craft-maker Sarah Maynard is second to play host in the Woking and Weybridge area, and hopes to impress her guests by serving up a three-course Turkish feast. However, the lavish spread needs a lot of preparation time, and she must hope it turns out as presentable as she plans it to be

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Riot policeman Steve Carvill hosts the third dinner party in Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, and hopes that a night of fine food and fun will impress his guests. He takes no prisoners with his menu, rustling up home-made pasta and white chocolate panna cotta from scratch for his four unsuspecting diners, who are expecting fast food rather than fine dining

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It is the turn of gardener Sara Gilding on the fourth night in Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, but it is her first time hosting a dinner party in more than 20 years. Guest Vincenzo Ferlita is not looking forward to the evening, while Steve Carvill hopes he will get some respite from Sara's flirtatious advances

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Children's entertainer Kelly Cummins hosts the final party from Woking and Weybridge in Surrey, and the winner of the £1,000 prize is announced. The guests keep their fingers crossed that dog-loving Sara Gilding does not return to her critical ways, while a bout of flatulence provides some comic relief

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to Newton House in Knaresborough, North Yorkshire, where Denise Carter and Jackie Fennell's traditional B&B focuses on organic, free-range breakfasts. Over dinner, guest Marianne Cammack and Deina Thomas from dominate the conversation, riling up some of the others, while Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson think the morning meal is all way too fancy

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to the Highlands Pub in Blackpool, where managers Mike Hayes and Neal Widdowson are all about value for money. However, Sean and Kathryn are horrified by what they find in their room, while Marianne and Deina think that theirs needs the female touch. In the afternoon the group competes in a basketball challenge at the amusement arcades, where the quiet hosts come out of their shells

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to the White House in Brighton for the third day of the competition, where hosts Sean Edlin and Kathryn Turnbull run a laid-back seaside B&B. Denise and Jackie think the rooms are not well equipped and Marianne finds them clinical, while Mike and Neal are unsure about the price. Tensions are temporarily alleviated by a visit to the famous Theatre Royal, but Marianne and Deina continue to cause trouble at dinner. In the morning, a locally sourced breakfast impresses some of the guests, but the group must decide whether they are getting value for money

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wakes Hall Lodges near Colchester is the fourth and final visit of the week, where Neal and Mike are wowed by the five-star self-catering accommodation, complete with hot tubs and lakeside views. The others aren't so sure about the 'luxury' breakfast hamper, however, especially as it means cooking their own. An afternoon of dog agility gives manager Marianne Cammack the chance to demonstrate her authority, although over dinner, her domineering conversation causes Jackie to finally snap

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. Mike and Neal from the Highlands Pub challenge the comments that they were considered dirty and lacking in facilities, while Sean and Kathryn from the White House are left smarting after suffering large underpayments for what they think are minor issues. Eventually, the winner is finally revealed

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick and Angel decide they will live as self-sufficiently as they can while restoring the chateau, and invest in some chickens to provide them with eggs for breakfast every morning. However, they still have countless problems to deal with, and have only £20,000 left to spend on the project. The chateau is still without either heating or water, while the 59 windows are crumbling. While Dick's renovations fall behind schedule, Angel is in her element coming up with ideas for what the finished building will look like

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick Strawbridge and Angel Adoree only have a few weeks to prepare for their wedding, but the chateau is nowhere near ready to receive the 200 guests they have invited and finding reliable, affordable workers is proving difficult. Dick oversees the damming of the moat and the installation of a new, eco-friendly sewage system, while Angel turns her attention to giving the rooms a distinctive decor

programme.title: A Taste of Italy
programme.desc: Nisha Katona explores the city of Sulmona in Abruzzo, uncovering the local specialties of the region and using their ingredients to create delicious lamb kebabs

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Documentary following the stories of three patients being treated for cardiac problems at St George's Hospital, south-west London. Corey has suffered cardiac arrest while playing ice hockey, prompting doctors to keep the 24-year-old in an induced coma to prevent further damage to his brain. At almost 60, Angie has a history of heart disease and her heart has been beating at over 150 for eight hours, risking stroke and other complications. Doctors also carry out tests on 49-year-old Steve to determine the cause of his dangerously high heart-rate

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: An hour in the life of paramedics across the country

programme.title: Surviving a Serial Killer
programme.desc: The personal stories of people who have survived face-to-face meetings with murderers. The first edition features Rhonda Stapley, who was attacked by Ted Bundy

programme.title: Murder in the Outback: The Falconio and Lees Mystery
programme.desc: Documentary examining the killing of British tourist Peter Falconio in Australia's Northern Territory in July 2001, exploring the details and questions that surround this infamous case. The concluding episode examines how DNA found on Joanne Lees' shirt and the couple's Kombi van were decisive in the murder trial of Bradley Murdoch, but even with this evidence, there are serious questions still to be answered in this controversial case

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Linden, 15, is brought to A&E having badly cut his leg while back-flipping off a shed roof onto a trampoline. As well as calling an ambulance, his friends filmed the accident on their mobile phones and happily show the clip to the staff at King's. Junior doctor Sammy looks after 88-year-old Panayiota, who has been referred to hospital with breathing difficulties. Originally from Cyprus, the patient speaks very little English and Sammy has to contact a translator to help with the diagnosis and treatment. Meanwhile, 55-year-old lorry driver Antony has lost all feeling in his left leg, and an ultrasound scan reveals he needs emergency surgery on a blocked artery to save his limb

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: An hour in the life of paramedics across the country

programme.title: Surviving a Serial Killer
programme.desc: The personal stories of people who have survived face-to-face meetings with murderers. The first edition features Rhonda Stapley, who was attacked by Ted Bundy

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The priests organise a raffle to raise funds to repair their leaky roof, planning to award a car as the prize. Unfortunately, Ted accidentally wrecks it before the grand draw takes place, and embarks on a frantic search for a replacement. Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon play the Craggy Island clerics
